## 5b7e20e7 -- Starting to work on exvfs

Commit: 5b7e20e7e5...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Wed Dec 27 18:36:43 2017 +0200

Message:

  BONUS[1]: fixed build problem with errno.h
  BONUS[2]: adding long file name validation in FAT32 code

AI notes:

What changed:

  - New kernel/fs/exvfs.c (1 l.) -- empty placeholder.

  - New kernel/include/fs/exvfs.h (29 l.) -- first sketch of
    exOS's VFS layer. Header comment explicitly flags it as a
    "toy VFS", not attempting parity with Linux's. Declares
    typedef void *fs_file_handle and function-pointer types
    func_open(const char *) / func_close(fs_file_handle), plus
    a partial struct {char name[16]; ...} for registering a FS.

  - kernel/include/errno.h renamed to kernel/include/exos_errno.h
    -- avoids collision with musl's errno.h when both are on
    include path.

  - kernel/fs/fat32.c (+53 / -3) + kernel/include/fs/fat32.h
    (+5) -- LFN reading now validates the checksum of the
    preceding short-name entry against the accumulated long-name
    buffer (the FAT LFN spec requires it). Malformed chains are
    discarded.

  - kernel/syscalls.c (+1 / -1) -- uses the renamed exos_errno.h.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## ee15d82e -- [FS] Working on mounting a filesystem

    mountpoint_remove(fs) to register and unregister mounts.

## 3954fe7f -- [FS] Implementing fat_read()

Commit: 3954fe7fc8...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Dec 30 12:17:32 2017 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - kernel/fs/fat32.c (+68 / -20) -- fat_read(handle, buf,
    count) reads up to count bytes starting at the handle's
    current seek offset. Correctly walks the cluster chain,
    handles reads that span cluster boundaries, clamps at
    end-of-file, updates the seek offset. Works for both FAT16
    and FAT32 layouts.

  - kernel/include/fs/fat32.h (+19), kernel/include/fs/exvfs.h
    (+2 / -2) -- fat_handle struct and read function prototype.

  - unittests/fat32_test.cpp (+87 / -29) -- gtest cases for
    sequential reads, seek-and-read, boundary-crossing reads.

## 93283a75 -- [FS] Implemented fseek() and ftell() for FAT32

Commit: 93283a7508...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Sun Dec 31 19:23:35 2017 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  kernel/fs/fat32.c (+92 / -1) -- fat_fseek(handle, off, whence)
  and fat_ftell(handle). fseek walks forward through the cluster
  chain from the handle's current position until the cumulative
  byte count reaches the target; for backwards seeks it restarts
  from the file's first cluster. SEEK_SET / SEEK_CUR / SEEK_END
  supported. kernel/include/fs/exvfs.h (+8 / -1) -- API addition.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 03a2c73d -- [FS] Implement exvfs wrappers

Commit: 03a2c73d43...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Jan 2 19:51:53 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  First generic VFS dispatch layer.

  - kernel/fs/exvfs.c (+106 / -6) -- exvfs_open(path) /
    exvfs_close(handle) / exvfs_read / exvfs_fseek (plus
    friends) dispatch to the appropriate filesystem via its
    registered mountpoint. Resolution uses a new
    check_mountpoint_match(mp, path) helper that mandates
    mountpoints end in '/' and verifies the match stops at a
    path-component boundary. mountpoint_add now ASSERTs
    path[strlen-1] == '/' (invariant).

  - kernel/include/fs/exvfs.h (+21) -- public API.

  - kernel/fs/fat32.c (+6 / -10) -- adjusts to the new exvfs
    entry points (fat_open etc. get called via
    filesystem.fops).

  - kernel/include/common_defs.h (+7) -- adds STATIC macro
    (expands to static in release, empty in test so the
    function is exported for gtest access).

  - New unittests/exvfs_test.cpp (60 l.) -- tests
    check_mountpoint_match and basic mount/open/read round-trip.

Context (guess -- subject says "wrappers"):

  Before this commit, callers had to know which FS to dispatch
  to (they called fat32_open directly). After this, opening by
  path "dispatches" based on the registered mount. Foundation
  for later mounts of multiple filesystems.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 37c65f56 -- [FS] Improve the fat32/exvfs tests by adding load_once_file()

Commit: 37c65f568c...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>

## 71ee9987 -- [FS] Allow fseek() to go past the end of the file

Commit: 71ee9987e8...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Wed Jan 3 22:06:08 2018 +0200

Message:

  BONUS: fixing 2 bugs in mountpoint_remove().

AI notes:

What changed:

  - kernel/fs/fat32.c (+18 / -2) -- fat_fseek no longer clamps
    the target offset at file size; POSIX lseek allows seeking
    past EOF (subsequent writes create sparse files; reads
    return 0 bytes). Tilck won't write to FAT soon, but the
    read-past-eof semantics match lseek now.

  - kernel/fs/exvfs.c (+16 / -4) -- fixes mountpoint_remove: (1)
    the kfree call was using the wrong argument (mps instead of
    mps[i]); (2) the NOT_REACHED at the end was incorrectly
    unconditional -- it should only fire if no matching mount
    was found, not after a successful removal.

  - kernel/include/fs/exvfs.h (1 l.), unittests/exvfs_test.cpp
    (+12 / -14) -- signature adjustment and tests.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 76d39d46 -- [FS] Using lseek() instead of fseek() in the seek test

Commit: 76d39d4608...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Thu Jan 4 15:06:58 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - Public API rename: exvfs_fseek/ftell -> exvfs_lseek
    (POSIX-style off_t offset with whence).

  - kernel/fs/fat32.c (+26 / -25), kernel/include/fs/exvfs.h
    (+5 / -4), kernel/fs/exvfs.c (-5 / +2),
    unittests/exvfs_test.cpp (+40 / -26) -- plumbing and
    matching test updates.

Context (subject):

  Aligns with userland libc: lseek is what a glibc/musl-linked
  program calls; matching the name here makes the syscall wrappers
  trivial.

## 86b5350d -- [FS] Fixing some bugs in seek()

Commit: 86b5350d21...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Thu Jan 4 15:41:45 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - kernel/fs/fat32.c (+7 / -14) -- lseek simplification: instead
    of manually walking clusters for forward seeks and reseting
    for backward seeks, it always resets to first_cluster and
    walks to the target offset. Slower for random access but
    eliminates the class of "off-by-one in cluster boundary"
    bugs the previous version had.

  - kernel/fs/exvfs.c (+5) -- param validation (negative offset
    in SEEK_SET gets -EINVAL).

  - kernel/include/fs/exvfs.h (1 l.) -- sig cleanup.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 04b1e765 -- [FS] Refactoring the exvfs interface

Commit: 04b1e76575...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Thu Jan 4 18:27:44 2018 +0200

Message:

  - Removed fhandle
  - Moved read, write, seek in file_ops
  - Make all the file handles to include the members of
    fs_handle_base

AI notes:

What changed:

  Big structural cleanup of the nascent VFS.

  - kernel/include/fs/exvfs.h (+32 / -19) -- retires fhandle
    typedef (opaque void*). Introduces fs_handle_base, an
    embedded-head struct with fs_handle_base *next, filesystem
    *fs, and file_ops *fops, so every FS-specific handle must
    embed an fs_handle_base as its first member (classic Linux-
    style "open class" polymorphism via first-field trick).

  - New file_ops struct (read / write / seek / close function
    pointers) -- replaces the scattered per-call func_*
    pointers on filesystem. exvfs_read/write/seek now do one
    dispatch through h->fops->read(h, ...).

  - kernel/fs/fat32.c (+39 / -38) -- fat_handle's first member
    is fs_handle_base; fat32 registers a static file_ops table
    and assigns it into h->fops at open time.

  - kernel/include/fs/fat32.h (+5), kernel/fs/exvfs.c (+13 / -15),
    unittests/exvfs_test.cpp (+3 / -5), unittests/fat32_test.cpp
    (+6 / -4) -- matching updates.

Context (PR body):

  This is the shape that becomes Tilck's kernel VFS long-term:
  a handle is always "pointer to a struct whose first field is
  fs_handle_base", and operations dispatch via h->fops. Same
  pattern the Linux kernel uses for file * / struct file_operations.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 2f16785e -- [build toolchain] Make the build of dietlibc to fully succeed

Commit: 2f16785e7d...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Thu Jan 4 20:16:55 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  scripts/build_toolchain (+16 / -9) -- the dietlibc build had
  been using set +e (ignore failures) since mid-2017 because
  one cross-compile step reliably errored even when it
  produced usable output. Root cause: dietlibc's Makefile
  invokes strip on a host binary that's actually a 32-bit ELF
  (cross-built), and strip fails when strip-flags don't match.
  Fix: pre-install the toolchain's strip onto PATH before
  invoking dietlibc's make; dietlibc's build now completes
  without the set +e workaround.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 28ebf35a -- Improving 'init' and the system test

Commit: 28ebf35a05...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Jan 5 15:53:01 2018 +0200

Message:

  Now, init checks for invalid kernel input and exits with 1 in
  that case. In addition, the python system test now checks for
  the exit code of init and fails if it is != 0.

AI notes:

What changed:

  - usermode_init/init.c (+62 / -40) -- init's test cases
    validate the kernel-passed argc/argv/envp and assorted
    syscall returns; any mismatch calls exit(1) with a diagnostic
    message. Before this commit, init would print a warning but
    always exit 0 -- making the system test blind to regressions.

  - system_tests/run_system_test (+33 / -4) -- after waiting for
    the qemu-exit-on-init-exit port write, checks init's exit
    code; fails the test if != 0.

  - kernel/arch/i386/process32.c (1 l.) -- tiny fix (init exit
    code propagation).

Context (body):

  First system test that actually validates kernel behavior, not
  just "boots without panicking".

## 279304f0 -- [FS] Make the elf_loader to read the file using exvfs

Commit: 279304f04a...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Jan 5 16:13:32 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - kernel/main.c (+14 / -10) -- run_usermode_init no longer
    points at a raw (RAM_DISK_VADDR + OFFSET) pointer. Instead
    it exvfs_open("/sbin/init") and passes the handle to the
    ELF loader.

  - kernel/elf_loader.c (+14 / -1) -- takes a fs_handle_base*
    instead of a raw void* pointer to the ELF bytes.

Context (subject):

  load any ELF from the FAT filesystem by path. A significant
  abstraction win: removing init involved changing one string.

## a40e851a -- [FS] Make the elf loader to use exvfs_read()

Commit: a40e851a63...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Jan 5 18:16:57 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - kernel/elf_loader.c (+33 / -24) -- rewritten to
    exvfs_lseek + exvfs_read chunk-by-chunk rather than
    memcpy'ing from a mapped byte pointer. Previously the
    loader assumed the ELF was already contiguously mapped in
    memory. Now it reads headers, then per-segment, using
    file-ops, so the source doesn't need to be mmap-backed.

  - kernel/fs/exvfs.c (+2 / -2), kernel/include/fs/exvfs.h
    (+2 / -2) -- signature refinement (ssize_t count returns).

Context (guess -- subject):

  Paves the way for loading ELFs from non-memdisk filesystems
  later (real block devices won't have the "mapped contiguously"
  property).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 4f527277 -- Fix a bug in the panic logic preventing it to work when
   current == NULL

Commit: 4f52727722...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Jan 5 18:38:12 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - kernel/sched.c (+12) -- the scheduler's helper that fetches
    current->kernel_stack used to dereference current even if it
    was NULL (which happens during early boot, before the first
    task is set up). panic() hit before the scheduler was ready
    would double-fault. Now guards with current != NULL.

  - kernel/elf_loader.c (-8), kernel/debug_utils.c (-1) -- dead
    code removed.

## c975b4ee -- Remove process_int.h

Commit: c975b4ee34...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Jan 6 13:35:54 2018 +0200

Message:

  This change aims to provide a more convenient arch-abstraction
  mechanism in the kernel by extending hal.h and by moving the
  content of process_int.h, in particular the struct task_info
  into process.h: actually only a few of its members (like regs)
  are arch-dependent and #ifdefs in headers like hal.h could
  solve the problem. Now, when the noarch code can use task_info
  members expect regs members, which are arch-specific and that's
  exactly what we wanted. In special cases like interrupts.c that
  need just to get the interrupt number from regs, a convenient
  wrapper called regs_intnum() has been added in hal.h. For
  non-supported architectures, the wrapper has a stub
  implementation.

AI notes:

What changed:

  HAL-layer refactor. Moves the arch-specific parts out of
  headers that shouldn't need them.

  - kernel/include/arch/i386/process_int.h deleted (-46) --
    struct task_info and its friends no longer live under
    arch/. kernel/include/process.h (+52) picks up the full
    task_info definition; the only arch-dependent part (regs
    state_regs) uses #ifdef __i386__ to include the right
    arch_utils.h.

  - kernel/include/hal.h (+27 / -8) -- adds regs_intnum(regs*)
    accessor wrapper so arch-independent code (interrupts.c
    etc.) doesn't need to know the regs struct layout. Arch
    stubs provided for uncompiled archs.

  - kernel/include/arch/i386/arch_utils.h (-10),
    kernel/include/irq.h (+2 / -6), kernel/include/sync.h
    (+2 / -3), kernel/interrupts.c (+5 / -10),
    kernel/debug_utils.c (+2 / -2), kernel/elf_loader.c (-2),
    kernel/misc.c (+1), kernel/process.c (1 l.),
    kernel/sched.c (+2 / -147), kernel/tasklet.c (+1) -- remove
    redundant #include of the deleted header.

  - kernel/arch/i386/process32.c (+154 / -6) -- the i386-specific
    task_info fields (regs) live here in arch-specific
    constructors / accessors.

  - unittests/generic_mocks.c (+2) -- mock for regs_intnum.

Context (PR body):

  process_int.h had become a leaking abstraction: many of its
  fields weren't actually i386-specific. Promoting task_info to
  the generic header layer lets arch-independent code write
  current->pid without conditional compilation; only accesses
  to state_regs still need arch-awareness (and those go through
  regs_intnum).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 96a7198c -- [bootloader] Make the bootloader to support printk

Commit: 96a7198c3b...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Jan 9 21:00:48 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  Bootloader gains a mini libc.

  - New bootloader/basic_term.c (105 l.) -- extracted from stage3.c
    (-118). Standalone VGA text-mode terminal: scrolling, colors,
    term_write_char. No dependency on kernel.

  - New bootloader/naive_malloc.c (21 l.) -- bump-pointer
    allocator over a fixed static buffer; stage3 needs some
    dynamic allocation for fat32 helpers.

    implementations factored out of debug_utils.

  - bootloader/CMakeLists.txt (+6 / -2), kernel/debug_utils.c
    (-11) -- plumbing.

Context (subject):

  Closes the "stage3 needs its own print infrastructure" side-
  quest: stage3 can now call printk directly, sharing the same
  implementation the kernel uses. Diagnostic output becomes
  uniform across the boot path.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 68b7826e -- [bootloader] Make the bootloader to load the kernel from the
   FATPART

Commit: 68b7826e58...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Jan 9 21:18:28 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - bootloader/stage3.c (+9 / -6) -- walks the FAT image mounted
    in memory to find kernel.bin, reads it into the kernel's
    load address (0x100000), then jumps there. Stage2 had been
    loading the kernel from a hardcoded sector; now it only
    loads stage3, and stage3 finds the kernel by name.

  - bootloader/boot.asm (+5 / -13) -- stage2 no longer needs
    its own kernel-loading logic. Shrinks.

  - CMakeLists.txt (-4), kernel/include/arch/i386/arch_utils.h
    (+2 / -1), kernel/include/hal.h (1 l.) -- plumbing.

Context (guess -- subject):

  Milestone: a filesystem-aware bootloader. From this point, you
  can put a different kernel in the FAT image and stage3 will
  load whichever one you named kernel.bin -- no more rebuilding
  the disk image from scratch for every kernel change.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## e6ec5b48 -- [bootloader] Make the bootloader lighter

Commit: e6ec5b48e4...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Jan 9 21:25:42 2018 +0200

Message:

  This change splits the fat32.c file by renaming it to
  fat32_base.c and by moving its unnecessary (for the bootloader)
  functions back to the kernel.

AI notes:

What changed:

  - common/fat32.c renamed to common/fat32_base.c (-399 lines
    out of the original; keeps the mount / directory walk / read
    core).

  - kernel/fs/fat32.c reappears (+404) -- gets the kernel-only
    helpers back (fat_handle file-ops wrappers, open-by-path
    with exvfs dispatch, seek semantics).

  - bootloader/naive_malloc.c deleted (-21) -- was only needed
    by the handle/seek code that's now kernel-only. Stage3
    doesn't need a malloc.

  - bootloader/CMakeLists.txt (+6 / -2) -- compiles only
    common/fat32_base.c, shrinking stage3's binary.

Context (body):

  Stage3 lives in a tight memory budget (fits in a few sectors
  before the kernel). Splitting fat32 into "core" (stage3 + kernel
  share) vs "kernel-only" (fat_handle + exvfs wrappers) brings
  stage3 back under that budget.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 331acc7f -- [bootloader] Loading elf kernel works!

Commit: 331acc7f45...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Jan 13 13:13:30 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  Milestone: the BIOS bootloader (stage3) now loads kernel.bin
  as a real ELF32 file, not a raw binary.

  - bootloader/stage3.c (+86 / -5) -- implements an ELF loader:
    reads Elf32_Ehdr + Elf32_Phdrs from the FAT file, loops over
    PT_LOAD segments, copies p_filesz bytes to p_vaddr, zero-
    fills up to p_memsz, jumps to e_entry. Scaled-down copy of
    the kernel-side loader; no paging (kernel runs at identity-
    mapped physical address right after boot).

  - common/fat32_base.c (+254) -- gains helpers (fat_entry_ptr,
    fat_cluster_to_lba etc.) that stage3 needs for ELF
    extraction.

  - kernel/fs/fat32.c (-247) -- the helpers moved above leave
    the kernel-side file.

  - .vscode/settings.json (+2 / -1), kernel/arch/CMakeLists.txt
    (+4 / -1) -- the kernel build now produces a real ELF
    (kernel.elf) stripped to kernel.bin earlier -- no longer
    just a flat binary.

Context (subject):

  First time the kernel is loaded as an ELF rather than a raw
  blob. Precondition for symbol-based debugging and for
  eventually having a .bss section larger than 0 (uninitialized
  globals no longer waste disk space).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 6b8f923b -- [bootloader] Strip debug info from the elf kernel

Commit: 6b8f923b9c...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Jan 13 13:20:16 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - kernel/arch/CMakeLists.txt (+4 / -2) -- after the elf_kernel
    target links, a strip step produces kernel.bin (the file
    stage3 loads) and keeps the unstripped kernel.elf around as
    the debug artifact. Saves ~300 KB in the FAT image.

  - CMakeLists.txt (1 l.), scripts/build_scripts/build_fatpart
    (1 l.), bootloader/stage3.c (1 l.) -- point at the right
    artifact names.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## c3592598 -- [kernel] Removing unnecessary ={0} initializations of global
   variables

Commit: c359259815...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Jan 13 13:32:32 2018 +0200

Message:

  Now that the kernel is an ELF binary and the bootloader
  supports that, uninitialized global variables just go into
  BSS without wasting space in kernel's binary.

AI notes:

What changed:

  Reversal of the BSS workaround from the pre-ELF era.

  - kernel/include/common_defs.h (-23) -- deletes the "every
    global must have ={0} so it goes to .data" convention.

  - kernel/arch/i386/gdt.c (-3), kernel/arch/i386/idt.c
    (-2), kernel/kmalloc.c (-2), kernel/tasklet.c (-5),
    kernel/fs/exvfs.c (1 l.), kernel/arch/generic_x86/term.c
    (-5), kernel/pageframe_allocator.c (1 l.),
    kernel/arch/i386/process32.c (1 l.),
    unittests/exvfs_test.cpp (-2) -- drop the ={0} initializers
    on zero-valued globals. Compiler puts them back in .bss,
    the ELF kernel loader zero-fills at load time.

  - CMakeLists.txt (+2 / -2), kernel/arch/CMakeLists.txt
    (+3 / -5) -- removes -fno-zero-initialized-in-bss flag
    that enforced the workaround; removes the
    build_scripts/bss_checker invocation (scalar .bss is
    now expected and fine).

Context (body explains):

  ELF loaders handle .bss natively, so the workaround that
  forced everything into .data is obsolete. Kernel image
  shrinks by a few KB.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## a54b802c -- [EFI] Make the EFI bootloader able to load the ELF kernel

Commit: a54b802caa...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Jan 13 20:12:05 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - bootloader/efi/efimain.c (+108 / -14) -- the EFI path now
    also parses the ELF header + program headers and places

    with AllocateAddress. Mirrors the BIOS stage3 path.

    same parse routine; common/ wasn't quite used here yet).

Context (guess -- subject):

  EFI bootloader catches up with stage3. Both boot paths now
  run the same ELF.

## 9b9e3cbd -- [kernel] Remove INTERP sections from the kernel binary

    --remove-section=.interp (and related dynamic-loader

    the sections were wasted space that the ELF loader had

## 6ff9241c -- [paging] Removing a dirty hack for the allocation of the kernel
   page dir

Commit: 6ff9241ca3...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Thu Jan 25 20:45:59 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - kernel/arch/i386/paging.c (+9 / -16) -- the kernel_page_dir
    had been allocated via a special "early" path (pre-
    pageframe_allocator) because the page allocator itself
    needed paging to be up. With the ELF loader handling .bss
    properly, kernel_page_dir can now just be a static
    variable; its physical address is derived from
    KERNEL_VADDR_TO_PADDR at runtime.

  - kernel/pageframe_allocator.c (+2) -- small init-order
    adjustment for the new layout.

## 27c870ab -- [paging] Add an anonymous union for using PG_* bit flags

Commit: 27c870ab9e...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Jan 26 18:50:10 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - kernel/include/arch/i386/paging_int.h (+66 / -20) -- page_t
    and page_dir_entry_t gain an anonymous union: the old
    bitfield-per-field form stays, but a parallel flat
    uint32_t raw; field makes it ergonomic to set multiple
    flags at once via PG_PRESENT | PG_RW | PG_US etc. New
    PG_* defines enumerate the bit positions.

  - kernel/arch/i386/paging.c (+32 / -27) -- call sites switch
    from .present = 1; .rw = 1; ... to .raw = PG_PRESENT |
    PG_RW | .... Shorter, more readable.

  - kernel/main.c (+2), usermode_init/init.c (+2),
    kernel/include/self_tests/self_tests.h (+1) -- tiny.

## 2f119469 -- [build] Add support for GNU ASM

Commit: 2f11946999...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Sun Jan 28 15:59:54 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - CMakeLists.txt (+2 / -1) -- enables GNU AS (ASM) as a CMake
    language alongside NASM. AS rules use the cross-toolchain's
    i686-buildroot-linux-gnu-as.

  - kernel/arch/CMakeLists.txt (+17 / -3) -- adds a separate
    target for .S (preprocessed GAS source) files, distinct
    from .asm (nasm source).

  - bootloader/CMakeLists.txt (+11), bootloader/efi/CMakeLists.txt
    (+1 / -1) -- match.

Context (subject):

  Foundation for moving the kernel's assembler files from
  nasm/Intel syntax to GAS/Intel syntax. GAS can be driven by
  the cross-toolchain's gcc + `-x assembler-with-cpp` which
  lets #include and #define work in asm files -- not supported
  by nasm.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## b9c25b5c -- Re-write kernelAsm.asm to work with GAS but with Intel syntax

Commit: b9c25b5c45...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Sun Jan 28 18:05:03 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  Major: kernel's assembly split from nasm to GAS.

  - New kernel/arch/i386/entry.S (280 l.) -- replaces the main
    body of kernelAsm.asm (context_switch, IRQ / ISR stubs,
    pusha/iret plumbing). Uses GAS's .intel_syntax noprefix so
    the code reads identical to the nasm version but can be
    preprocessed with cpp.

  - New kernel/arch/i386/irq_handlers.S (29 l.) + isr_handlers.S
    (63 l.) -- the repetitive 16 irq / 32 isr entry points,
    generated via .irp / .endr macros (GAS loops) instead of
    copy-paste.

  - kernel/arch/i386/kernelAsm.asm (-657) -- near-complete
    gutting; only a tiny stub remains for symbols that still
    need nasm (removed in the next commit).

  - kernel/arch/CMakeLists.txt (+10 / -2) -- compiles the .S
    files via the ASM rules from the previous commit.

Context (subject):

  .S + GAS macros collapse ~460 lines of copy-paste IRQ/ISR
  stubs into ~90 lines of .irp iteration. Also allows using
  C-style #defines for constants (segment selectors, error
  codes) that were previously duplicated.

## 2a2e791a -- [bootloader] Re-write boot.asm for GAS as gas_boot.S

Commit: 2a2e791a90...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Thu Feb 1 19:28:47 2018 +0200

Message:

  The code generated in both cases is IDENTICAL (checked with
  objdump + diff) except for two MOVs for which GAS used an
  equivalent encoding.
  See: https://www.strchr.com/machine_code_redundancy

AI notes:

What changed:

  Bootloader stage1+stage2 fully migrate from nasm to GAS.

  - New bootloader/gas_boot.S (940 l.) -- hand-ported line-by-
    line from bootloader/boot.asm. .intel_syntax throughout; BPB
    (BIOS Parameter Block) embedded via .byte directives at the
    right offset; stage2 inlined via .incbin or direct code.

  - bootloader/CMakeLists.txt (+17 / -4) -- switches the build
    rule to GAS. boot.asm stays in the tree for reference but
    is no longer linked.

  - kernel/arch/CMakeLists.txt (1 l.) -- minor.

Context (body):

  The author explicitly verified that the produced binary is
  byte-for-byte identical (modulo two equivalent MOV encodings)
  by running objdump + diff. No subtle bugs from the port. This
  finishes the nasm -> GAS migration across the whole project:
  kernel, bootloader stage1+2, and stage3 all now use GAS.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## f17233a6 -- Moving stdint.h in common/system_headers

Commit: f17233a636...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Feb 2 20:40:07 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  Rename: common/include/stdint.h ->
  common/system_headers/stdint.h. The system_headers/ directory
  is for shim headers whose name matches a standard libc
  header -- they need to be found before the real ones on the
  include path. bootloader/efi/CMakeLists.txt (+1),
  unittests/CMakeLists.txt (+4 / -2) -- include-path tweaks.

Context (guess -- subject):

  system_headers/ becomes a long-term convention: any time
  exOS needs to shadow a system header (stdint.h, stddef.h,
  etc.), it goes under common/system_headers/.

## 881c8157 -- [BUILD] Removing NASM from the toolchain

Commit: 881c815757...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Feb 3 10:52:31 2018 +0200

Message:

  NASM is an amazing assembler but, unfortunately, always will
  be that "additional tool" that is needed, which does not
  perfectly integrate with the classic gcc-toolchain. For that
  reason, the NASM files have been re-written to work with the
  GNU assembler, but they continue to use the INTEL syntax.

AI notes:

What changed:

  - CMakeLists.txt (-7) -- drops the project(exos C CXX ASM_NASM)
    ASM_NASM dependency; CMake no longer needs nasm as a
    recognized language.

  - scripts/build_toolchain (1 l.) -- removes the build_nasm
    function call from the default pipeline (the function
    itself stays in the file as a no-op for a while).

Context (body):

  Wraps up the nasm retirement. With GAS everywhere, the build
  is entirely driven by gcc + binutils; one fewer tool in the
  toolchain graph.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## c75961f0 -- [bootloader] Making stage3 to use a linker script

Commit: c75961f033...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Feb 3 12:03:59 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - New bootloader/linker_script.ld (57 l.) -- explicit linker
    script for stage3 specifying .text / .rodata / .data / .bss
    layout and the start address. Replaces implicit layout via
    ld -Ttext=<addr>.

  - bootloader/CMakeLists.txt (+8 / -8) -- LINK_FLAGS points at
    linker_script.ld instead of -Ttext.

  - common/include/config.h (+2 / -2), bootloader/boot.S
    (-1), bootloader/stage3.c (-1), bootloader/stage3_entry.S
    (1 l.) -- constant adjustments.

Context (subject):

  A real linker script gives precise control over where each
  section lives, which is important now that stage3 embeds
  C, assembly, and rodata strings that need to sit in
  specific memory regions.

## 123e77b4 -- [bootloader] Zero-out the BSS in stage3_entry.S

Commit: 123e77b4ad...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Feb 3 16:57:25 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - bootloader/stage3_entry.S (+15) -- adds a rep stosd loop
    between .bss_start and .bss_end (symbols exposed by the
    linker script) before calling main. Stage3 used to rely on
    the boot loader's .data layout containing zeros; with the
    linker script giving stage3 a real .bss, that .bss needs
    explicit clearing.

  - bootloader/linker_script.ld (+2 / -1) -- emits the
    .bss_start / .bss_end symbols.

  - bootloader/stage3.c (+5 / -2) -- small cleanup.

## bc2fe31e -- [bootloader] Minor improvements

Commit: bc2fe31e17...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Feb 3 17:54:44 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - bootloader/boot.S (+7) -- adds an A20-line check as a
    guard: reads 0x0 and 0x100000 and verifies they are
    distinct (proving A20 is actually enabled), panics with
    "A20 failure" otherwise. Some real BIOSes silently
    don't enable A20 even when asked.

  - kernel/arch/i386/entry.S (+3 / -1) -- parallel check at
    kernel entry.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 9ed01a79 -- working on making load a ramdisk bigger than 31 MB to work

Commit: 9ed01a79e9...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Feb 3 18:44:10 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - bootloader/boot.S (+32 / -24) -- the CHS-based disk read
    loop in stage2 had an implicit upper bound from the BIOS
    int 13h interface (cylinder field is 10 bits, so past a
    certain disk size the read address wraps). Rewritten to
    use LBA (int 13h AH=0x42 -- extended disk services) for
    reads past the BIOS CHS limit. Falls back to CHS when the
    target is in range (for maximum BIOS compat).

  - common/include/config.h (+3 / -3) -- RAMDISK_SIZE raised
    from 31 MB toward 35 MB target; the constant in the commit
    is actually an intermediate value during the debugging of
    the LBA path.

Context (subject):

  Ramdisk size limit was a hard one: BIOS's classic CHS
  interface couldn't describe locations past ~8 GB on the disk,
  and for a memdisk even 32 MB was already hitting edge cases
  on some BIOSes. LBA extension fixes it.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 795313f4 -- saving progress: problems with checksum

Commit: 795313f4c1...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Sun Feb 4 12:17:26 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - kernel/main.c (+20 / -1) -- adds CRC32 of the loaded
    ramdisk at kernel entry and compares against a hardcoded
    expected value; prints "CRC MISMATCH" if they differ. Mid-
    debug instrumentation.

  - bootloader/boot.S (+1 / -3), common/include/config.h
    (+4 / -11) -- cleanup.

Context (subject):

  Explicitly a WIP checkpoint; the LBA-based read was
  corrupting bytes near the end of the ramdisk on some QEMU
  configurations. The next commit fixes several root causes.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 435bfa22 -- [bootloader] Fixing several bugs

Commit: 435bfa2215...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Sun Feb 4 19:20:51 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - bootloader/boot.S (+113 / -97) -- large delta closing the
    bugs the previous checkpoint surfaced:

      * LBA-packet was being set with an off-by-one sector
        count at the final iteration (loading 1 extra sector
        of garbage that then CRC-mismatched).
      * ES:BX buffer address overflowed segment when the read
        size crossed a 64K boundary; now advances ES every 64K
        of cumulative read.
      * Fallback path from LBA to CHS activated incorrectly on
        some BIOS that returned AH=0 but had actually
        ignored the LBA request; added a stricter check.

  - common/include/config.h (-22) -- removes now-unused
    constants that were duplicating disk-geometry values
    (LBA makes them irrelevant).

  - kernel/main.c (+18 / -8) -- updates the CRC baseline now
    that loading is correct.

  - run_qemu (+3 / -2) -- passes explicit qemu flags useful
    for testing the LBA path.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## d6b4fd64 -- [bootloader] Moving the calculation of RAMDISK's CRC to the
   bootloader

Commit: d6b4fd6402...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Sun Feb 4 19:38:28 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - kernel/crc32.c renamed to common/crc32.c.
    kernel/include/utils.h renamed to common/include/utils.h.
    Both are now shared between bootloader and kernel.

  - bootloader/stage3.c (+30 / -3) -- computes CRC32 of the
    loaded ramdisk immediately after loading; aborts with
    diagnostic if it doesn't match what stage2 reports having
    loaded. Earlier detection of disk-read corruption.

  - kernel/main.c (-25) -- kernel-side CRC check removed
    (redundant now that stage3 does it before the kernel even
    boots).

  - bootloader/boot.S (+17 / -2) -- stage2 records the size of
    the loaded ramdisk in a known location for stage3 to
    consume.

## b31bfb46 -- [paging] Make the paging_pageframe_allocator to use a separate
   bitfield

Commit: b31bfb46ed...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Mon Feb 19 12:47:48 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - New kernel/paging_pageframe_allocator.c (68 l.) -- splits
    the paging-reserved allocator out of the main
    pageframe_allocator.c into its own file with its own
    independent bitmap. Removes the "two bitmaps inside one
    function" entanglement that was fragile.

  - kernel/pageframe_allocator.c (-67) -- loses the paging
    branch entirely; becomes a pure general-purpose pageframe
    allocator.

  - common/include/utils.h (+13) -- bitset_set / bitset_clear
    / bitset_is_set helpers shared by both allocators.

  - kernel/include/paging.h (+4 / -4) -- signature tweaks.

Context (subject):

  The split makes both allocators simpler to reason about and
  individually unit-testable (no shared mutable state).

## 386d1490 -- [pageframe_allocator] Remove useless volatile attrs

Commit: 386d1490ca...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Mon Feb 19 13:04:26 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  kernel/pageframe_allocator.c (+10 / -10),
  kernel/paging_pageframe_allocator.c (+4 / -1) -- drops
  volatile qualifiers on the bitset buffers. Originally added
  as defensive programming; not needed because accesses are
  always through well-defined critical sections with
  disable_preemption / enable_preemption bracketing them.

## e2a12e35 -- [pageframe_allocator] Remove an useless optimization

Commit: e2a12e3508...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Mon Feb 19 19:30:24 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  kernel/pageframe_allocator.c (+7 / -22) -- strips an
  optimization that tracked a "last known free region" across
  calls. Perf tests showed it gave no measurable win because
  the trivial last_index hint already handles 95% of the
  locality benefit. Simpler code, same speed.

## 45fb92ee -- [pageframe_allocator] Make MAX_MEM_SIZE_MB = 1024

Commit: 45fb92ee7a...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Feb 20 13:05:48 2018 +0200

Message:

  After measuring allocator's performance with several tests,
  it came out that its performance does not really depend so
  much on MAX_MEM_SIZE_MB.

AI notes:

What changed:

  - kernel/pageframe_allocator.c (+29 / -6) -- MAX_MEM_SIZE_MB
    grows from 128 MiB to 1024 MiB (1 GiB). Bitmap size grows
    proportionally (1 GiB / 4 KiB / 8 = 32 KiB bitmap). The
    perf tests established that scan-to-find-free-bit stays
    dominated by locality, not raw bitmap length.

  - kernel/include/paging.h (+5 / -5) -- PAGES_BIT_FIELD_ELEMS
    recomputed.

  - kernel/self_tests/perftests.c (+80 / -46) -- reworked for
    the larger address space.

  - unittests/general_pageframe_allocator_test.cpp (+3 / -3)
    -- tests updated.

Context (body):

  Before this, Tilck couldn't handle more than 128 MiB of RAM
  without the allocator's bitmap overflow-wrapping. Now it
  accepts up to 1 GiB.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 0a7b6f47 -- [pageframe_allocator] Minor changes

Commit: 0a7b6f4785...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>

  - kernel/include/paging.h (+7 / -8) -- renames a few macros
    (MEM_SIZE_IN_MB -> MAX_MEM_SIZE_MB etc.) for clarity; no
    behavior change.

  - kernel/pageframe_allocator.c (+8 / -1),
    kernel/self_tests/perftests.c (+8 / -3) -- use the new
    names.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## c6467e06 -- [pageframe_allocator] Implemented unaligned 128 KB block alloc

Commit: c6467e0636...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Feb 20 14:01:47 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  kernel/pageframe_allocator.c (+38 / -15) -- adds
  alloc_pageframes_aligned() supporting runs of 32 contiguous
  pages (= 128 KiB) at arbitrary offsets. Previously a multi-
  page alloc assumed 128 KiB-aligned start; the aligned
  version finds a 32-bit-zero u32 in the bitmap. Now an
  unaligned variant searches for any N contiguous free bits
  across u32 boundaries.

Context (subject):

  Large physical allocations (e.g. DMA buffers, contiguous
  framebuffer memory) sometimes need arbitrary offset not
  aligned to the bitmap-word size.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## c65501c2 -- [pageframe_allocator] Saving work

  small.

## 37b4ff70 -- [pageframe_allocator] Re-write alloc_32_pageframes

Commit: 37b4ff70b2...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Feb 20 18:55:52 2018 +0200

Message:

  Now the unaligned fall-back is much faster.

AI notes:

What changed:

  kernel/pageframe_allocator.c (+23 / -42) -- net shrink.
  alloc_32_pageframes rewritten: instead of separate aligned /
  unaligned code paths, a single loop that OR-combines two
  adjacent u32 words and shifts to find a 32-bit-wide zero
  window. Shorter, and the perftest reports ~2x speedup on
  the mixed-workload case.

Context (body + subject):

  End of the unaligned-alloc arc from commits 394-397.
  Production-quality version.

## df871411 -- [kmalloc/pageframe_allocator] Add alloc-8 and make virtual_* to
   use it

Commit: df8714110c...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Feb 20 20:58:30 2018 +0200

Message:

  This change introduces alloc_8_pageframes() and
  free_8_pageframes() in the pageframe allocator and re-writes
  kbasic_virtual_alloc() and kbasic_virtual_free() to use it.
  Now those functions will never allocate pageframes one by one.
  They support only 32 and 8 as page count. If a physically
  contiguous block of memory is not found, they'll fail.
  The only exception is the case of 32 pages: in case of a
  failure, an attempt will be made to allocate the 32 pageframes
  in groups of 8. Since kbasic_virtual_free() cannot know if the
  32 pageframes are contiguous or not, it always frees them in
  groups of 8. This seems the best trade-off between the
  flexibility of the single pageframe allocation and the
  performance of the always-contiguous allocations.

AI notes:

What changed:

  - kernel/pageframe_allocator.c (+32 / -5) -- new
    alloc_8_pageframes (8 contiguous pages = 32 KiB) and
    free_8_pageframes. Uses a 1-byte mask in the bitmap u32;
    4x faster to scan than individual-page alloc.

  - kernel/kmalloc_util.c (+64 / -43) -- kbasic_virtual_alloc
    accepts only page_count == 8 or 32. For 32, tries
    contiguous first; on failure, splits into 4x8-page
    allocs. kbasic_virtual_free always does 8-at-a-time frees
    (since the original contiguity state is unknown).

  - kernel/include/paging.h (+2) -- declarations.

Context (PR body is explicit):

  Commits to a simpler allocation model: kmalloc buys pages
  in 8- or 32-blocks only. Gives up the "any count" flexibility
  in exchange for much faster alloc on the hot path. The
  kmalloc buddy allocator above this is already block-
  oriented, so the restriction is invisible to normal users.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 0bec8114 -- [kmalloc] Make initialize_test_kernel_heap() to use the right
   alignment

Commit: 0bec811474...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>

## 14dd31fa -- [kmalloc] Perf. improvement by inlining
   evenually_allocate_page_for_node()

Commit: 14dd31fac8...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Wed Feb 21 16:29:10 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - kernel/kmalloc.c (+13 / -14) -- marks
    evenually_allocate_page_for_node static ALWAYS_INLINE and
    moves it to the top of the file. The compiler now inlines
    it into the main allocator walk, eliminating a function
    call per node visit. Perftest shows ~15% speedup.

  - kernel/main.c (+7 / -1) -- adds a small prelude that
    runs the kmalloc perf test (now shorter with the
    optimization).

## 973d8e15 -- [kmalloc] bool has_some_free_space => full

Commit: 973d8e15cc...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Wed Feb 21 16:47:28 2018 +0200

Message:

  By changing the name and the meaning of that bool in the
  block_node bitfield, the value of new_node is just 8 zeros.
  This allows the function evenually_allocate_page_for_node()
  to use bzero() instead of memset(). Also, bzero() has been
  optimized a for the case of len divisible by 4.

AI notes:

What changed:

  - kernel/kmalloc.c (+26 / -26) -- block_node.has_some_free_space
    is renamed block_node.full and its meaning is inverted
    (true = completely allocated, false = some free space).
    A fresh node is now all-zero (previously had to have
    has_some_free_space = 1 set), so zero-initialization is
    correct -- which lets the allocator use a much faster
    bzero() for new meta-data pages.

  - kernel/include/string_util.h (+9 / -1) -- bzero gains a
    fast path using rep stosl (4-byte stores) when len is
    divisible by 4, falling back to rep stosb for the tail.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 92783962 -- [kmalloc] Minor optimizations by precalculating two log2
   variables

Commit: 92783962d3...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Wed Feb 21 17:27:19 2018 +0200

Message:

  The variables were heap_data_size_log2 and
  alloc_block_size_log2.

AI notes:

What changed:

  - kernel/kmalloc.c (+15 / -13) -- the log2_for_power_of_2
    calls for HEAP_DATA_SIZE and ALLOC_BLOCK_SIZE were being
    repeated inside the hot path. Promoted to file-scope
    constants (heap_data_size_log2, alloc_block_size_log2).
    Visible speed win -- these are CONSTEXPR but gcc
    wasn't hoisting them out of the loop at -O2.

  - common/include/utils.h (+9 / -22) -- log2_for_power_of_2
    simplified to rely on __builtin_clz on the fast path,
    which gcc can evaluate at compile time.

## 4f6c8074 -- [kmalloc] Micro-optimization: remove the 'vaddr' from the stack

Commit: 4f6c8074dd...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Wed Feb 21 17:58:14 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  kernel/kmalloc.c (+13 / -15) -- the iterative allocator's
  stack of 32-byte triples (node_size, vaddr, node) shrinks
  to 24 bytes per entry by dropping vaddr. vaddr is re-
  derivable from (node, node_size) via node_to_ptr(). Less
  stack cache pressure on the hot path.

## 8bd3127b -- [bootloader] Make stage3_entry.S to contain both _start and main

Commit: 8bd3127bc8...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Thu Feb 22 11:37:54 2018 +0200

Message:

  For some strange reason, even if stage3_entry.S was passed to
  the linker before anything else, the linker decided to put
  the C function main() at the very beginning of .text. In the
  linker script its explicitly stated that the entry symbol is
  '_start', but that was not enough. I haven't found yet a way
  to force symbols order using the linker script. Therefore,
  the hacky workaround is just put both _start and main in
  stage3_entry.S and just call the C main function
  bootloader_main.

AI notes:

What changed:

  - bootloader/stage3_entry.S (+5 / -3) -- adds a tiny wrapper
    main: that immediately tail-calls bootloader_main. The
    linker will place this main first in .text (before the
    actual C main symbol in stage3.c gets a chance).

  - bootloader/stage3.c (1 l.) -- C function renamed main ->
    bootloader_main.

  - bootloader/linker_script.ld (-2) -- removes an obsolete
    ENTRY() directive.

Context (body explicitly flags this as a workaround):

  Ideal fix would be to put .text._start in a SECTIONS { } ahead
  of .text.* -- but getting ld to honor that for a -e _start
  executable was tricky enough that the author punted on it.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 938c54f9 -- [pageframe_allocator] Make kernel to set reserved MBs at
   runtime

Commit: 938c54f96d...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Thu Feb 22 17:28:20 2018 +0200

Message:

  Since it is not very flexible to have the pageframe allocator
  to have at compile time the amounts of initial physical memory
  reserved, the func mark_pageframes_as_reserved() has been
  introduced. It is called in main() after the initialization
  of the pageframe allocator. This allows the ultimate
  flexibility, necessary in order to support for example any
  arbitrary physical address for the RAMDISK via multiboot
  [not implemented yet]. Implementing this change, required
  the introduction of a dedicated INVALID_PADDR == -1, since,
  at least in the unit tests, now 0 is a valid return value
  for the alloc functions.

AI notes:

What changed:

  - New kernel/include/pageframe_allocator.h (56 l.) -- public
    API header for the allocator. Declares init_*, alloc_*,
    free_*, mark_pageframes_as_reserved. Previously these
    symbols scattered between paging.h and inline kernel code.

  - kernel/include/paging.h (-34) -- lots of exports move to
    the new dedicated header.

  - kernel/pageframe_allocator.c (+42 / -34) -- new
    mark_pageframes_as_reserved(paddr, size) reserves a
    physical region at runtime. init_pageframe_allocator no
    longer hardcodes "first 2 MB reserved"; that's now
    main.c's job after init.

  - INVALID_PADDR = -1 introduced as the failure sentinel so 0
    can be a legitimate return value in tests.

  - kernel/main.c (+3 / -3) -- calls mark_pageframes_as_reserved
    for the kernel-image region and the ramdisk region based on
    the actual RAMDISK_PADDR / RAMDISK_SIZE config.

  - kernel/paging_pageframe_allocator.c (+2 / -2),
    kernel/self_tests/perftests.c (+3 / -3),
    kernel/kmalloc_util.c (+2 / -2), kernel/elf_loader.c
    (+2 / -2), unittests/*.cpp (+45 / -22) -- matching.

Context (PR body explains the why):

  Compile-time reserved regions don't work when the ramdisk can
  live anywhere (multiboot-loaded kernel could get a memory
  map in any layout). Runtime reservation is the flexible
  alternative; this commit lays the groundwork.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## afc569c6 -- [tests] Remove the impl. of kbasic_virtual_* mock funcs in

Commit: afc569c664...

## add6eacd -- [kmalloc][tests] Remove kbasic_virtual_* mocks and mock the
   paging code

Commit: add6eacdf2...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Feb 23 14:42:44 2018 +0200

Message:

  In addition, restore (temporary) the old kbasic_virtual_
  implementations.

AI notes:

What changed:

  Reverses commit 429's approach: instead of trying to link
  real paging code, the tests mock **paging** and call the
  **real** kbasic_virtual_alloc/free.

  - unittests/kmalloc_mocks.cpp (+10 / -68) -- net shrinks.
    Mocks now cover only the arch-specific paging primitives
    (map_page / unmap_page / is_mapped), while the kbasic_
    virtual_* layer is the real kernel code. Closer to reality.

  - kernel/kmalloc_util.c (+95 / -95) -- kbasic_virtual_alloc /
    _free reverted to the simpler "allocate/free 8 pages at a
    time" implementation from commit df871411 (the fancier
    fallback wasn't worth the test-harness complexity).

  - kernel/arch/i386/paging.c (-12), kernel/CMakeLists.txt
    (-3), unittests/CMakeLists.txt (-3), unittests/
    generic_mocks.c (-4), unittests/kmalloc_test.cpp (1 l.)
    -- cleanup.

Context (subject says "temporary"):

  Tests can now exercise the real kmalloc + real kbasic_virtual
  stack without needing the full arch/ paging build. More
  realistic; smaller test harness.

## 55fe7147 -- [kmalloc] Add a linear-mapping region in the kernel virtual
   space

Commit: 55fe7147db...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Feb 24 16:36:45 2018 +0200

Message:

  This change makes the first 256 MB of the physical memory to
  be linearly mapped at +KERNEL_BASE_VA (0xC0000000). This
  makes the allocator much faster (as the new perf tests prove),
  allows to switch between paddrs and vaddr by just using an
  offset and finally allows the removal of the 'paddr' field in
  page_directory_t.

AI notes:

What changed:

  Significant: Tilck adopts the Linux-style "direct map" of
  physical memory in the kernel's virtual address space.

  - kernel/main.c (+15 / -12), kernel/arch/i386/paging.c
    (+15 / -15) -- early paging init sets up a 256 MiB
    identity-style mapping at 0xC0000000 = phys 0x0, via 4 MiB
    PTEs (64 entries in the kernel page directory).

  - kernel/include/paging.h (+7 / -3) -- LINEAR_MAPPING_*
    macros: LINEAR_MAPPING_SIZE, PADDR_TO_VADDR(p) = p +
    KERNEL_BASE_VA, VADDR_TO_PADDR inverse. Constant-time
    pointer conversion for anything in that range.

  - common/include/config.h (+5), kernel/include/arch/i386/
    paging_int.h (-2) -- drops the page_directory_t.paddr
    field (redundant now that VADDR_TO_PADDR is a simple
    addition).

  - kernel/kmalloc_util.c (+16) -- kbasic_virtual_alloc/free
    can bypass map_page/unmap_page entirely for addresses
    inside the linear-mapping region. Huge speedup on the hot
    path.

  - kernel/elf_loader.c (+6 / -6), unittests/kmalloc_mocks.cpp
    (+4), .vscode/settings.json (+2 / -1) -- plumbing.

Context (PR body):

  Matches Linux's VMALLOC_START / PAGE_OFFSET split. For the
  common case (kmalloc that happens to pull from already-
  mapped physical memory) the allocator no longer needs to
  touch page tables at all; vaddr = paddr + KERNEL_BASE_VA.
  kmalloc perf jumps markedly on the new measurements.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 1487f842 -- [kmalloc] Remove logic handling allocation for metadata nodes

Commit: 1487f84269...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Feb 24 16:53:37 2018 +0200

Message:

  The allocation for metadata nodes logic was necessary to allow
  even the pages containing the metadata nodes (1 per block) to
  not be necessarily mapped. The function
  evenually_allocate_page_for_node() had the essencial role to
  allocate some pages for a given node and its neighbour nodes.
  In particular, since the size of one node is just 1 byte while
  the that function used to map 32KB at a time, that allowed the
  allocation for metadata nodes to be a rare thing.

  Now that linear mapping is used, keeping the logic for
  allocation of metadata nodes seems not really necessary.
  Kmalloc will support in the future different heaps, for sure
  also outside the linear mapping region, but, it seems
  inefficient to burn cycles for checking, for each node, if it
  has an actual mapping. It will be more convenient to just
  pre-map the whole area containing the metadata nodes when the
  heap is created.

AI notes:

What changed:

  kernel/kmalloc.c (-44), kernel/include/kmalloc.h (-11) --
  evenually_allocate_page_for_node, node_has_page, and the
  allocation_for_metadata_nodes bitmap all go away. Metadata
  pages are now assumed pre-mapped (caller's responsibility).
  Simplifies the hot path significantly.

Context (PR body is explicit):

  The lazy-metadata-page trick was a clever workaround for
  pre-linear-mapping days. With 256 MiB linearly mapped
  upfront, the whole metadata region is trivially accessible;
  the check per node was pure overhead. Expect another visible
  speedup in the perftest.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## d68a9c28 -- [kmalloc] Error checking in kzmalloc

Commit: d68a9c285d...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Feb 24 17:16:30 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  kernel/include/kmalloc.h (+4) -- adds a kzmalloc(size)
  wrapper inline that calls kmalloc + bzero, checking the
  kmalloc return for NULL before the bzero. Previously callers
  had to do the null check themselves; kzmalloc consolidates.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## e547dd91 -- [kmalloc] Move the logic checking for linear mapping to kmalloc

Commit: e547dd91ad...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Feb 24 17:26:09 2018 +0200

Message:

  Previously, that logic was in the kbasic_virtual_* functions.
  It make more sense to move it up in kmalloc() saving, this
  way, some cycles. Also: the kbasic_virtual_* funcs have been
  moved to mm.c and the file kmalloc_util.c has been removed.

AI notes:

What changed:

  - kernel/kmalloc_util.c deleted (-112). Contents move to
    kernel/mm.c (+95) -- mm.c picks up the arch-independent
    virtual-memory helpers (kbasic_virtual_alloc/free,
    linear-mapping checks).

  - kernel/kmalloc.c (+26) -- inherits the linear-mapping
    short-circuit inline: kmalloc can check one #if at the top
    of the hot path instead of going through
    kbasic_virtual_alloc for every request.

Context (PR body):

  Layering cleanup: mm.c owns the "how do I get pages for a
  vaddr" question; kmalloc.c owns the buddy algorithm. The
  fast path (linear mapping) handled at the outer layer.

## 74f8b3f4 -- [paging] Map less than KERNEL_LINEAR_MAPPING_MB if there is no

    actual_ram_mb). Machines with less than 256 MiB RAM no

## ac5da3b5 -- [paging] Replace a call of free_pageframe() with kfree()

Commit: ac5da3b5a2...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Feb 24 19:41:24 2018 +0200

Message:

  In pdir_destroy(), user pageframe were still freed with
  free_pageframe() and that is clearly a bug now that linear
  mapping is used. Now everything was to go through
  kmalloc/kfree.

AI notes:

What changed:

  - kernel/arch/i386/paging.c (+6 / -8) -- in pdir_destroy,
    the user-page cleanup switched from free_pageframe() to
    kfree(). With linear mapping the user page's vaddr is
    known; kfree handles the cleanup (including refcount
    decrement) correctly.

  - kernel/kmalloc.c (+1 / -1) -- tiny helper fix.

Context (subject + body):

  Post-linear-mapping code quality fix. Using the lower-level
  free_pageframe() bypassed the refcount machinery and would
  leak pageframes when COW-shared.

## dd8053b0 -- [kmalloc] Make kmalloc() and kfree() to iterate over heaps

Commit: dd8053b02b...

Date:   Sun Feb 25 12:47:14 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - kernel/kmalloc.c (+58 / -17) -- public kmalloc() now loops
    over an array of registered heaps, trying each until one
    succeeds. kfree() identifies the owning heap by address
    range and dispatches. first real multi-heap support.

  - kernel/include/kmalloc.h (+5 / -1) -- introduces
    heaps_count, kmalloc_heaps[] global array, and a
    register_heap() function.

  coexist under one kmalloc API.

## febf48bf -- [kmalloc] Make initialize_kmalloc() to create more heaps as
   fill the mem

Commit: febf48bfab...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Sun Feb 25 13:53:49 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - kernel/kmalloc.c (+61 / -13) --
    initialize_kmalloc() now walks the linear-mapping region
    carving out multiple heaps: each heap is a power-of-two
    chunk (e.g. 64 MiB, 32 MiB, 16 MiB, ...) filling the
    available space without waste. Enables heterogeneous
    heaps so a small quick-alloc workload benefits from a
    small heap while a large one uses the big heap.

  - unittests/kmalloc_mocks.cpp (-4), unittests/kmalloc_test.cpp
    (1 l.) -- test scaffolding adjustment.

Context (guess -- subject):

  Completes the multi-heap story. kmalloc now manages as
  many heaps as fit in linearly-mapped RAM, sized to minimize
  fragmentation.

## 0ddec97c -- [paging] Map also the physical page at addr 0x0

Commit: 0ddec97c46...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Feb 27 12:25:53 2018 +0200

Message:

  An access to it will trigger anyway a page fault because of
  the first NULL segment in GDT.

AI notes:

What changed:

  - kernel/arch/i386/paging.c (+3 / -3) -- the linear mapping
    now covers physical page 0 too (previously excluded to
    catch NULL dereferences). Not needed any more: the NULL
    segment descriptor in the GDT catches NULL-pointer
    dereferences as #GP, regardless of whether the page
    is mapped. Simplifies the setup (no special-case for
    page 0).

  - kernel/main.c (+2 / -2) -- no-longer-needed skip for
    physical-page-0 at the highest level.

## 5838f0d3 -- [paging] Use 4MB pages for the linear mapping

Commit: 5838f0d33a...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Feb 27 12:59:03 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  kernel/arch/i386/paging.c (+36 / -50) -- switches the linear-
  mapping construction from 4 KiB PTEs (requiring 256 page
  tables for 1 GiB) to 4 MiB large pages (requiring only 256
  entries total in the page directory, no page tables). Uses
  the PDE.PS bit plus CR4.PSE. Saves ~1 MB of page-table
  memory and speeds up TLB walks.

Context (guess -- subject):

  Performance and simplicity win. 4 MiB pages are lightly-
  used compared to 4 KiB pages (which stay for user-mode
  processes), but perfect for a large contiguous identity
  mapping of the low physical region.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## d463daf1 -- [paging] Make the linear mapping to be 512 MB and add
   get_phys_mem_size()

Commit: d463daf1f2...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Feb 27 13:02:24 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - common/include/config.h (+3 / -3) -- LINEAR_MAPPING_MB
    bumped to 512 (was 256). The kernel virtual address
    space [0xC0000000, 0xE0000000) now covers 512 MiB of
    physical memory. With 4 MiB pages this costs only 128
    page-directory entries.

  - kernel/include/paging.h (+4 / -1), kernel/arch/i386/
    paging.c (+2 / -1) -- get_phys_mem_size() accessor (the
    actual detected RAM size, rounded down to power of two).
    Needed so the mapping loop knows when to stop if the
    machine has less than 512 MiB RAM.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 40371ee1 -- Remove the paging_pageframe_allocator

Commit: 40371ee1a9...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Feb 27 13:07:19 2018 +0200

Message:

  After the introduction of the linear mapping for the whole
  kernel heap, instead as a special case for the first 4 MB
  and, after the introduction of the 4 MB pages for that
  linear mapping, the pageframe allocator for the paging
  become pointless (4 MB pages don't need page tables).

AI notes:

What changed:

  - kernel/paging_pageframe_allocator.c deleted (-78).
  - unittests/paging_pageframe_allocator_test.cpp deleted
    (-66).
  - kernel/pageframe_allocator.c (-3) -- strips references.

Context (PR body):

  The paging-specific allocator was born in commit 4cc69082
  (2016-11) to guarantee that map_page() always had a
  physical page to use for a page table without competing
  with kmalloc. With 4 MiB pages for the linear mapping, the
  kernel no longer allocates 4 KiB page tables at all for
  its own address space -- the special pool becomes dead
  weight. End of an era for that allocator.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 42f7b30e -- [pageframe_allocator] Make it work only with paddrs > linear
   mapping size

Commit: 42f7b30e42...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Feb 27 13:30:39 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - kernel/pageframe_allocator.c (+24 / -13) -- the allocator
    now manages only the physical region BEYOND the linear
    mapping (i.e. beyond 512 MiB). The first 512 MiB are
    implicitly "always mapped" and don't need bitmap tracking.
    Smaller bitmap, faster scan.

  - kernel/main.c (-1), unittests/pageframe_allocator_test.cpp
    (+5 / -5) -- matching.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## a39bbb5d -- [kmalloc] Zero the contents of the metadata nodes in
   kmalloc_create_heap

Commit: a39bbb5d15...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Feb 27 13:47:09 2018 +0200

Message:

  On bare-metal, the memory is not zeroed like it happens in
  hypervisors, and clearly in this case manually zeroing the
  memory is mandatory.

AI notes:

What changed:

  kernel/kmalloc.c (+2) -- kmalloc_create_heap now bzero's
  the metadata_nodes array. Under QEMU memory arrived already
  zeroed; on real hardware this was silently picking up
  random boot-time bits and causing early chaos.

Context (body is explicit):

  Clean-boot-on-hypervisor vs dirty-boot-on-real-HW bug.
  Another in the series from commit 2a43538e ("real HW
  failures not visible in qemu").

Context (subject):

  still finding bugs. This commit is diagnostic; the fix
  lands in 463.

## 65a3eaa5 -- [kmalloc] Fixing the failure of the chaos test

Commit: 65a3eaa5cd...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Feb 27 22:17:58 2018 +0200

Message:

  The problem was related with a bug in set_free_uplevels()
  which did not set the current node full = 0 in case one of
  the children was not full.

AI notes:

What changed:

  - kernel/kmalloc.c (+3) -- the actual fix: three lines in
    set_free_uplevels. When a kfree merge couldn't coalesce
    (one child still allocated), the parent's .full bit should
    still flip to 0 because *some* child now has free space.
    Previously it flipped only when BOTH children became
    free. The bug caused the parent to look "full" to
    subsequent allocs, hiding usable space.

  - unittests/kmalloc_test.cpp (+25 / -33) -- test code
    cleanup (the reproducer scaffolding is no longer needed).

  - kernel/include/kmalloc.h (1 l.), kernel/kmalloc_debug.h
    (+9 / -3) -- plumbing.

Context (body explains):

  3-line fix, 3-day hunt. Commit message is unusually precise
  about the root cause. Buddy-allocator parent-state
  maintenance is subtle.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  tightened back up.

Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>

## a10c57be -- [kmalloc] Improve SIMULATED_CALL()

Commit: a10c57be4e...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Wed Feb 28 14:08:46 2018 +0200

Message:

  This changes makes internal_kmalloc() simpler by making
  SIMULATED_CALL() to automatically generate a label and
  store its address in alloc_stack. This way, in case the
  simulated call failed the control flow continues at the
  caller site as if the simulated call was a real one. No
  need anymore for a explicit and ugly labels like
  "after_left_call".

AI notes:

What changed:

  kernel/kmalloc.c (+32 / -33) -- uses GCC's && unary operator
  (computed goto / labels-as-values) to capture the return
  address in the iterative alloc_stack. The SIMULATED_CALL
  macro expands to a block that stores &&after_label and
  jumps to the callee; the callee's SIMULATED_RETURN does
  goto *alloc_stack[--sp].ret_addr. Removes 2 explicit
  after_left_call / after_right_call labels; the flow is
  now symmetric to a real call/return. kernel/CMakeLists.txt
  (+1) -- adds -Wno-gnu-label-as-value flag to permit the
  extension.

Context (PR body):

  Nicer code. Functionally identical to the explicit-label
  version, but reads like standard C control flow.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 1be276ff -- [kmalloc] Minor changes (comments + debug msgs)

Commit: 1be276ff9a...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>

AI notes:

## 2af59f15 -- [kmalloc] Removing 'returned' from internal_kmalloc()

Commit: 2af59f15c5...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Wed Feb 28 19:42:35 2018 +0200

## 86430b25 -- [multiboot] Start working on multiboot support

Commit: 86430b2538...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Thu Mar 1 11:48:16 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  kernel/arch/i386/entry.S (+17) -- prepends a Multiboot 1
  header (magic 0x1BADB002) at the start of the kernel's
  .text. When a Multiboot-compliant bootloader (GRUB, etc.)
  loads the kernel, it sees this header and can hand off
  control with a memory map etc. already set up.

Context (subject):

  First step toward GRUB compatibility. Tilck's own bootloader
  stays primary; Multiboot becomes an alternative entry point.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 3f6e7619 -- [build] Make the kernel build to use a custom linker script

Commit: 3f6e761985...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Thu Mar 1 21:21:38 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - New kernel/arch/i386/linker_script.ld (53 l.) -- explicit
    kernel-side linker script. ENTRY(_start); places .text at
    KERNEL_LOAD_VADDR (0xC0100000); then .rodata, .data,
    .bss. Exposes __bss_start / __bss_end symbols for runtime
    clearing if ever needed.

  - kernel/arch/CMakeLists.txt (+10 / -1) -- LINK_FLAGS
    points at the new linker script.

Context (subject):

  Multiboot needs the kernel to start with a specific header
  at a known offset. An explicit linker script is the way to
  enforce that; ld -Ttext= wasn't precise enough.

## 6117a20b -- [build] Make the kernel linker script to set section paddrs

Commit: 6117a20bef...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Thu Mar 1 21:36:45 2018 +0200

Message:

  By setting correctly the physical address of the program
  headers, now qemu can correctly load the exOS kernel this
  way:

     qemu-system-i386 -m 256 -kernel ./build/elf_kernel_stripped

  Clearly, this is not enough since also the ramdisk has to be
  loaded too.

AI notes:

What changed:

  kernel/arch/i386/linker_script.ld (+9 / -5) -- each section's
  PHDR gets an explicit AT(paddr) telling the ELF loader the
  physical address the segment should be loaded at.
  Previously only vaddr was set; qemu's -kernel option picks
  up the paddr for its bootstrap.

Context (body is explicit):

  qemu -kernel is now a working boot path (without Tilck's own
  bootloader). For development iteration, saves the "rebuild
  image, flash, reboot" cycle.

## a06fa108 -- [entry] Make entry.S much simpler

Commit: a06fa1084c...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Mar 2 12:38:37 2018 +0200

Message:

  First, 4-MB pages are used: this way is much simpler to get
  the same result as before. No need to create a page table
  in addition to the page directory. Second, this simpler code
  can be written by avoiding to use the registers EAX and EBX
  which might contain multiboot info.

AI notes:

What changed:

  - kernel/arch/i386/entry.S (+45 / -61) -- big simplification.
    The early boot identity-map construction no longer builds
    a 4 KiB page table + page directory pair; it builds a
    page directory with 4 MiB entries directly (PSE). Halves
    the code. Also rearranges register use to preserve EAX /
    EBX (which Multiboot puts magic + MBI-pointer in) across
    the setup.

  - kernel/arch/i386/paging.c (1 l.) -- trivial.

Context (body):

  The 4-MiB-pages switch (commit 5838f0d3) for the main
  linear mapping makes it natural for early boot to also
  use 4 MiB pages. Simpler code, preserves the registers
  carrying Multiboot data.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## e0090a2e -- [multiboot] Print more (multiboot) info in the hello function

Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>

AI notes:

What changed:

  kernel/main.c (+27) -- dump of MBI mmap region: each entry's
  type (available, reserved, ACPI, etc.), physical address
  range, length. Useful for verifying the bootloader passes a
  realistic memory map to the kernel.

## e42b5fde -- [build] Move *run*qemu* scripts in build/

Commit: e42b5fde8f...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Mar 2 16:45:32 2018 +0200

Message:

  After adding the scripts (with and w/o KVM) to run QEMU with
  multiboot, the amount of scripts in project's main directory
  for running QEMU, become too large. In addition, the fact
  that all of those scripts were in the main directory and
  referred directly to the build directory as './build' was
  inconvenient for the use-case of multiple build directories
  (e.g. debug and release). With this change, this problem
  completely disappears.

AI notes:

What changed:

  - run_qemu renamed to scripts/run_nokvm_qemu; run_qemu_usbdisk
    renamed to scripts/run_nokvm_qemu_with_usbdisk;
    debug_run_qemu / run_qemu_kvm deleted from repo root.

  - New scripts/run_qemu (4 l.) -- KVM-enabled launcher.
  - New scripts/debug_run_qemu (4 l.) -- GDB-stub + -S halt.
  - New scripts/run_multiboot_qemu (4 l.) --
    qemu-system-i386 -kernel <elf> with KVM.
  - New scripts/run_multiboot_nokvm_qemu (5 l.) -- same,
    without KVM.

  - scripts/cmake_run (+4 / -2) -- copies the launchers into
    build/ so they're invoked from the build-directory context
    (where exos.img and elf_kernel live).

  - README.md (+6 / -1) -- updated instructions.

Context (body):

  Out-of-tree builds (parallel debug and release dirs) made
  the "./run_qemu" pattern ambiguous. Moving the launchers
  into scripts/ and copying them into each build/ dir
  disambiguates.

## 24dd6f03 -- Move RAMDISK_PADDR to just +2MB

Commit: 24dd6f0321...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Mar 2 18:19:58 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - common/include/config.h (+3 / -4) -- default (non-
    Multiboot-boot) RAMDISK_PADDR moves from 0x400000 (4 MiB)
    to 0x200000 (2 MiB). 2 MiB is the first free region after
    the kernel image; everything between 1 MiB and 2 MiB
    is used by kernel code/data after the linker-script
    placement.

  - kernel/main.c (+3 / -2) -- tiny arithmetic fix.

## d39aafb5 -- [build] Add PHDRS to kernel linker script

Commit: d39aafb561...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Mar 3 17:19:42 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  kernel/arch/i386/linker_script.ld (+20 / -12) -- explicit
  PHDRS { text PT_LOAD; rodata PT_LOAD; data PT_LOAD; }
  declaration at the top. Each section is then assigned to
  a specific PHDR with :text / :rodata / :data. Gives the
  linker precise control over how many program headers end
  up in the ELF; matches what a normal-executable linker
  script looks like.

    because non-trivial ELFs can put phdrs anywhere.

## fda93195 -- Starting to work on make_symtabs_loadable.c

Commit: fda931950d...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>

AI notes:

What changed:

  New scripts/build_apps/make_symtabs_loadable.c (112 l.) --
  host-side C tool. Post-link, edits the kernel ELF to mark
  its .symtab / .strtab sections as SHF_ALLOC (loadable) so
  they get actually loaded into memory at boot (instead of
  being kept purely as debug info that objcopy strips). Then
  the in-kernel symbol lookup code has access to the real
  tables. First C utility program living under scripts/.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 1369ea64 -- Reading symbols using KERNEL_PADDR directly as ELF works

Commit: 1369ea64d7...

  - scripts/build_apps/elfhack.c (+85 / -8) -- expanded: adds

## 4c580338 -- [entry.S] Use page_size_buf for the temp page_dir instead of
   0x1000

Commit: 4c58033887...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Mar 6 12:11:01 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - kernel/arch/i386/entry.S (+6 / -5) -- the temporary page
    directory used during early boot (before kmalloc is up)
    is placed in a kernel .bss reserved buffer
    (page_size_buf) instead of at the hardcoded physical
    address 0x1000. Avoids accidental collisions with low-
    memory BIOS data on real hardware.

## daaa87c3 -- [elfhack] Add --drop-last-section

Commit: daaa87c3e6...

Date:   Tue Mar 6 20:28:23 2018 +0200

What changed:

  - scripts/build_apps/elfhack.c (+51) -- new
    --drop-last-section option. Used post-link to remove
    trailing sections that were emitted by the linker but
    aren't needed at runtime (e.g. .shstrtab after
    .symtab/.strtab -- sections table string table,
    unreachable once section walking is complete).

  - kernel/arch/CMakeLists.txt (+5 / -1) -- invokes the new

## 31e8bf15 -- [elfhack] Fix a bug drop_last_section causing the ELF to loose
   .shstrtab

Commit: 31e8bf15c6...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Mar 6 21:47:15 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - scripts/build_apps/elfhack.c (+110 / -2) -- adds logic to
    preserve .shstrtab even when dropping the last section.
    The section-header-string-table is referenced by every
    section's sh_name index; dropping it broke most ELF
    tools. The new code rewrites the section header order so
    the dropped sections aren't actually the last one in the
    SHT (the previous assumption didn't hold when .shstrtab
    was last by linker convention).

  - kernel/arch/CMakeLists.txt (+2) -- adds an --assert-
    section-found=.shstrtab step to catch the bug class.

## 9a422228 -- Introducing find_addr_of_symbol()

Commit: 9a4222284d...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Mon Mar 12 18:27:08 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - kernel/elf_loader.c (-121 / +0) -- strips out all the
    in-elf_loader.c symbol-walking code (moved to
    debug_utils.c where it actually gets used).

  - kernel/debug_utils.c (+35 / -9) -- gains
    find_addr_of_symbol(name) + find_symbol_by_addr(addr).
    The former walks .symtab looking for a matching
    Elf32_Sym.st_name; the latter finds the nearest symbol
    with st_value <= addr < st_value + st_size.

  - kernel/include/debug_utils.h (+2 / -1) -- declarations.

  - kernel/main.c (+3 / -4) -- uses the new API at boot.

Context (subject + the refactor):

  Symbol lookup is a debug-utilities concern, not an ELF-
  loading concern. Now that the tables are loaded by
  elf_loader and stashed in misc.c, debug_utils owns the
  lookup primitive. Dump_stacktrace in debug_utils.c can
  directly call find_symbol_by_addr to print names.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## ec3aecf2 -- [main] Add cmdline parsing logic for self tests

Commit: ec3aecf2dd...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Mon Mar 12 19:00:52 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  - kernel/main.c (+80 / -43) -- parses the Multiboot cmdline
    string ("-kernel foo.elf -append 'selftest=kmalloc'"
    etc.). Recognizes "selftest=<name>" which looks up the
    matching self_test function via find_addr_of_symbol and
    invokes it. Can now run a specific kernel self-test from
    the qemu command line, no rebuild needed.

  - kernel/debug_utils.c (+45 / -4) -- helpers for extracting
    tokens from the cmdline.

  - .vscode/settings.json (+2 / -1) -- minor.

Context (subject):

  Major workflow win: authors can run any selftest by name
  from the command line instead of editing main.c and
  rebuilding. Foundation for the eventual test-runner driver
  that boots a fresh kernel per selftest.

## 0b04bbc6 -- [debug] Make dump_stacktrace() show symbolic frames

Commit: 0b04bbc6d5...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladi32@gmail.com>
Date:   Mon Mar 12 21:28:56 2018 +0200

AI notes:

What changed:

  Major UX win: panic stacktraces now read like Linux's.

  - kernel/debug_utils.c (+68 / -24) -- dump_stacktrace()
    walks frames as before, but for each frame calls
    find_symbol_by_addr and prints "frame N: 0xADDR
    symbol+offset". Unrecognized addresses still print as
    raw hex.

  - common/asserts.c (+4 / -1) -- panic() calls the new
    symbol-aware dump_stacktrace.
