## 593073db -- [kmalloc] Use common/norec.h to avoid recursion

Commit: 593073db2a...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Apr 6 14:25:28 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  +48 / -89 across 2 files. kernel/kmalloc.c -47 net: its
  recursive buddy-tree walk deleted in favour of norec.h.
  norec.h gets +4 net (small API polish).

Context (guess -- from subject):

  The buddy allocator's split/merge operations were recursive
  walks of its buddy tree. With ~16KB kernel stacks, a deeply
  fragmented allocator could blow up. Switching to the
  explicit-stack walker makes kmalloc stack-safe.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## d2aed0ca -- [threads][step 19] Implement create_new_pid() in a
   more efficient way

Commit: d2aed0caf3...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Apr 6 18:05:08 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  +55 / -9 across 2 files. sched.c +52 net: create_new_pid()
  now uses a bitmap / AVL ordered by tid to find the first
  free pid quickly instead of linear-scanning the task tree.
  avl_bintree.c +3 (helper).

Context (guess -- from subject):

  Linear search for next free pid becomes O(n) as tasks
  accumulate. Bitmap lookup (or tree gap-finding) is O(log n)
  or amortised O(1).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  `access = DPL0 | TYPE_CODE | PRESENT`, replacing magic
  0x9A / 0x92 / 0xFA / 0xF2 constants.

Context (guess -- from subject):

  Payoff of 7c6eb9fc: readable, auditable GDT entry
  setup. When something breaks, the flag is obvious.

## 577852e3 -- [interrupts] Use a separate code path for IRQs

Commit: 577852e35d...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Sun Apr 8 12:12:55 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  +52 / -19 across entry.S, irq_handlers.S, isr_handlers.S,
  interrupts.c. IRQ dispatch no longer funnels through the
  shared ISR common stub; irq_handlers.S has its own common
  tail that calls a specialised handle_irq() in the C code.

Context (guess -- from subject):

  Exception and IRQ flows have different responsibilities
  (error codes vs PIC EOI) but were sharing a common prologue.
  Splitting lets each path omit code the other needs, at the
  cost of a touch of duplication.

## e2afccac -- [gdt] Introduce gdt_add_entry() and gdt_expand()

  +80 / -25 across 3 files. gdt.c +74 net: gdt_add_entry(...)
  finds a free slot or triggers gdt_expand() (grows the GDT
  by reallocating, copies over entries, reloads via lgdt).
  gdt_int.h exposes +5 lines of new decl. hal.h drops 1.

Context (guess -- from subject):

  GDT slot. Fixed-size boot GDT can't accommodate that.

## 699136ec -- [gdt] Add bitfields to gdt_entry

Commit: 699136ec76...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Apr 10 21:47:42 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  +29 / -3 in gdt_int.h. gdt_entry struct gains explicit
  bitfields for the access byte and flags nibble (present,
  dpl, type, granularity, size, ...).

Context (guess -- from subject):

  Instead of constructing descriptors by bit-ORing into a
  u64, callers can now write `e.present = 1; e.dpl = 0;`.
  Complements the macro-flag work but catches more
  mistakes at compile time.

## 134e2865 -- [ldt] Preparations for LDT

Commit: 134e2865c5...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Wed Apr 11 13:10:36 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  +86 / -5 across 8 files. process.h +7: task_info grows a
  per-task LDT pointer. gdt.c +18 net; gdt_int.h +4 (LDT
  descriptor support). ldt.c +16: LDT load/init. process32.c
  +34: switch_to_task now loads per-task LDT. process.c +5
  (init in allocate).

Context (guess -- from subject):

  LDT is x86's per-task descriptor table. The groundwork
  lets set_thread_area populate an LDT so multiple threads
  can have distinct TLS bases without fighting over GDT
  slots.

## 0a18c365 -- [interrupts] Don't validate stack ptr in case of
   faults

Commit: 0a18c36563...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Wed Apr 11 13:39:30 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  1 line in kernel/interrupts.c. The push_nested_interrupt
  stack-pointer sanity check is skipped for exception
  vectors (faults).

Context (guess -- from subject):

  Faults can fire with an unusual/corrupt stack (stack
  overflow, bad pointer deref); asserting on ESP bounds
  turns a recoverable fault into a double-panic. Fault
  handlers must tolerate weirdness.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## b0e87fce -- [ldt] Using load_ldt() in switch_to_task() does not
   cause GPF anymore

Commit: b0e87fce53...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Wed Apr 11 13:51:46 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  +6 / -13 across 4 files. Small adjustments in gdt.c,
  gdt_int.h, idt.c, process32.c. Exact fix probably
  involves re-ordering lldt vs segment-register reloads.

Context (guess -- from subject):

  Previous commit's LDT-load caused #GP. Subject phrasing
  ("does not cause GPF anymore") confirms this commit is
  the fix. Typical cause: loading an LDT selector before
  the LDT itself is installed, or DS/ES pointing into a
  now-invalid descriptor.

## 3919dc8e -- [set_thread_area] Add critical checks preventing user
   to crash the system

Commit: 3919dc8edc...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Wed Apr 11 20:35:01 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  +101 / -37 across 3 files. gdt.c +88 net: validation of
  every user_desc field (limit, base, flags combo) before
  installing into GDT. Rejects descriptors that would let
  userspace peek into kernel memory or escalate
  privilege. process32.c -0 net small refactor. process.h
  +1 new field.

Context (guess -- from subject):

  Security hardening. An unchecked set_thread_area is a
  textbook privilege-escalation primitive. Linux
  set_thread_area has identical checks (see arch/x86/
  kernel/tls.c in Linux).

## a0b0c0ef -- [threads] Implement set_tid_address()

Date:   Wed Apr 11 17:49:01 2018 +0300

  caller-provided clear_child_tid pointer. syscalls.c wires
  syscall 258 into the dispatch. process.h +4 adds field.

Context (guess -- from subject):

## 8c62f077 -- [gtests] Using C++'s features to remove the macros

Commit: 8c62f0772f...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Thu Apr 12 20:50:36 2018 +0300

AI notes:

  is very different -- preprocessor macros (used to
  generate per-type tests) replaced by C++ templates,
  type_traits, snprintf-based golden comparisons.

Context (guess -- from subject):

  Macros in C++ tests are a smell: they lose type info,
  confuse IDEs, and break debuggers. Templates
  accomplish the same fan-out with type safety.

## 6ffa275c -- [printk] Copying (+fork) its code from common/ to
   bootloader and kernel

Commit: 6ffa275c80...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Apr 13 12:57:43 2018 +0300

> This change is necessary in order to extend the kernel
> implementation of printk() without affecting the
> minimal one used for the bootloader. Its the same
> happened for 'term'.

AI notes:

What changed:

  +183 / -95 across 5 files. common/string_util.c -94:
  printk code leaves. New bootloader/basic_printk.c (97
  lines) and kernel/printk.c (85 lines) each own a
  copy. basic_term.h moves into the bootloader dir.
  stage3.c 1-line include adjust.

Context (from body):

  The common-code "printk with weak linkage" trick
  (commit 133dcb0b, step 6 of the header reorg) worked
  but kept kernel and bootloader tied to one source.
  Forking lets the kernel printk grow features (log
  levels, ring buffer, lock) without bloating the
  bootloader.

## db641bbf -- [sys_writev] Make it run with preemption disabled

Commit: db641bbfff...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Apr 13 13:08:24 2018 +0300

> At the moment there are still no filesystem locks in
> exOS, therefore just preemption is disabled. Still,
> sys_writev() needed the preemption to be disabled since
> it has to behave like a single write(). No other writes
> can interfere.

AI notes:

What changed:

  +7 / -2 in fs_syscalls.c. sys_writev brackets its iovec
  loop with disable_preemption / enable_preemption.

Context (from body):

  Atomicity: writev is supposed to be equivalent to a
  single write of the concatenated buffers. Without FS
  locks the only way to prevent interleaved writes from
  other tasks is to keep preemption off for the duration.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## a56eb056 -- [printk] Make it use a ringbuffer in case of nested
   [e.g. IRQ] calls

Commit: a56eb0566f...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Apr 13 19:15:18 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  +152 / -15 across 6 files. printk.c +113 net: adds a
  ring buffer; when printk is called while another printk
  is already executing (nested via IRQ), the new call
  appends to the ringbuf and returns. The outer call's
  flush step drains the ringbuf. interrupts.c +22:
  hook to trigger drain on IRQ return. kb.c, timer_handler
  emit marker prints to exercise the path. basic_defs.h
  +5.

Context (guess -- from subject):

  The term_write path is not re-entrant (holds no lock
  and manipulates cursor state). Without this, a printk
  from an IRQ handler interleaving with a concurrent
  printk would scramble the screen. Ringbuf defers the
  nested print until it's safe to flush.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## b68aca4e -- [printk] Fix a bug in printk_flush() by adding
   minibuf

Commit: b68aca4ece...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Apr 14 11:48:41 2018 +0300

> The problem: with the previous impl. we first claim in
> the do-while loop that we have read the data from the
> ringbuf and then we actually read it (while writing to
> term). The problem with that approach is that if another
> printk occurs (3rd nested call), it will find the used
> field = 0 and so will use up to sizeof(printk_rbuf) for
> appending its data, corrupting this way that the 2nd
> nested call was about to read. The solution is to use
> a minibuf in the do-while loop and, each time, copy the
> data safely on the stack. In the worst case when the
> loop has to iterate more then once, we'll just have to
> re-read the data.
>
> BONUS: this commit also adds term_write_char2(c, color)
> to term.h allowing its callers to write a char using a
> specific color without changing the current color.

AI notes:

What changed:

  +48 / -30 across 4 files. printk.c +27 net implements
  the minibuf fix: copy to a small stack buffer first,
  then write to term. term.c +10 net: new
  term_write_char2(c, color). term.h +1 decl.
  timer_handler.c 1-line adjust.

Context (from body):

  Classic TOCTOU on a single-producer/single-consumer
  ring. Fix is to read-then-commit, not commit-then-read.
  Commit message is a textbook example of capturing a
  subtle concurrency bug.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  occasional parallel-build ordering failures.

## 170f5134 -- Introduce copy_from_user() and make it to work in
   sys_write()

Commit: 170f51341d...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Apr 14 14:50:27 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  +97 / -12 across 7 files. New header exos/user.h (14
  lines) declaring copy_from_user / copy_to_user. New
  kernel/arch/i386/user.c (39 lines) with the i386
  impl that catches faults (probably via a fixup table
  checked in page_fault_handler). paging.c +12 / -4:
  page-fault handler recognises the fixup and returns
  an error. fs_syscalls.c switches sys_write to use
  copy_from_user. printk.c +4 / -2 likely uses it for
  %s. debug_utils.c +3 / -1.

Context (guess -- from subject):

  Foundational security primitive: kernel must never
  dereference user pointers without a mechanism to
  handle bad addresses. copy_from_user is the standard
  Unix idiom. This commit is the seed -- more syscalls
  converted in the following commits.

## 2ec124ce -- [sys_writev] Use copy_from_user() for iov

Commit: 2ec124cecb...

  the user pointer (easy bypass). After: every user

## c271d968 -- [process] Introduce per-task copybufs and make write
   and writev to use them

Commit: c271d96828...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Apr 14 19:46:18 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  +32 / -11 across 3 files. process.h +4: task_info gains
  a per-task kmalloc'd copy buffer. process.c +17 net
  allocates/frees it with the task. fs_syscalls.c
  switches sys_write / sys_writev to this buffer instead
  of stack / per-call kmalloc.

Context (guess -- from subject):

  Performance: avoid kmalloc on every syscall, avoid
  deep stack usage for user-copies. Per-task buffer is
  a good compromise (no locking required, reused across
  syscalls).

## 78cb4017 -- [process] Fix bug in allocate_new_process()

Commit: 78cb4017b0...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Sun Apr 15 12:25:30 2018 +0300

> pi->ref_count was set to 1 before the bzero() [!parent
> case]. Therefore, it dropped to 0 from the beginning and
> free_task() never hit the condition ref_count == 0.

AI notes:

What changed:

  +26 / -4 across 4 files. process.c +8 / -1: reorder
  the bzero() and ref_count=1. kmalloc.c +14: probably a
  new kzalloc() helper now that the "zero then init"
  ordering bug was spotted. kmalloc.h +1 decl.
  shell_commands.c 3-line tweak.

Context (from body):

  Textbook ordering bug: `pi = kmalloc(...); pi->ref = 1;
  bzero(pi, ...);` zeroes the just-set ref. Fix is to
  bzero first (or use kzalloc).

## 47f797c8 -- [process] Introduce and use free_mem_for_zombie_task()

Commit: 47f797c827...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Sun Apr 15 12:50:13 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  +22 / -12 across 4 files. process.c +18 net: a dedicated
  free_mem_for_zombie_task() frees the page dir + kernel
  stack of an already-dead task (zombie), run from a
  different context than the dying task itself. Used from
  sched.c (+2) after parent reaps the zombie, and from
  process32.c (+1).

Context (guess -- from subject):

  A task can't free its own kernel stack (it's running on
  it). Splitting the teardown into "task exits" (keep
  minimal resources as zombie) and "zombie is reaped"
  (drop the rest) follows the standard Unix model.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## f46681df -- [process] Make sys_exit() and kthread_exit() to free
   the kernel stack

Commit: f46681dfe4...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Sun Apr 15 14:20:38 2018 +0300

> This has been made possible thanks to the
> switch_to_initial_kernel_stack() assembly function, which
> just switches the stack pointer to the end of the initial
> stack area. The free of the kernel_stack is done by the
> call to free_mem_for_zombie_task().

AI notes:

What changed:

  +61 / -46 across 6 files. entry.S +12 net new asm
  switch_to_initial_kernel_stack. process32.c -6 net uses
  it. process.c +13 net: sys_exit / kthread_exit now call
  free_mem_for_zombie_task on themselves after jumping
  onto the bootstrap kernel stack. process.h +7, misc.c
  +8, config.h +1.

Context (from body):

  Continuation of 47f797c8. The final piece: a dying task
  can free its own kernel stack if it first moves off it
  onto the shared initial stack. Same idea as Linux's
  `die_task` path.

## fd0f406f -- [gtests] Fix build error (missing mocks)

Commit: fd0f406fea...

## 303a4a7a -- Introduce the KERNEL_TRACK_NESTED_INTERRUPTS config
   option

Commit: 303a4a7a28...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Sun Apr 15 21:14:52 2018 +0300

> This allows all the functions related with push/pop
> nested interrupts to just do nothing. All of its purpose
> is anyway only debug-related. Still, by default the
> option will be 1, but it can be disabled. Also, the
> function are_interrupts_enabled() has been made
> debug-only: there should be no non-debug logic relying
> on it.
>
> BONUS: fixing a bug in find_sym_at_addr().

AI notes:

What changed:

  +43 / -17 across 8 files. config.h +2 (option macro).
  interrupts.h +10 (stubs for disabled case). interrupts.c
  +24 / -14 gates push/pop nested interrupts behind the
  macro. elf.c 1-line fix (the "BONUS"). debug_utils.c,
  timer_handler.c, entry.S, x86_utils.h adjust.

Context (from body):

  Nested-interrupt bookkeeping has cost at every IRQ/
  syscall boundary; for debug it catches "this ran with
  interrupts disabled" bugs, but in release it's dead
  weight. Making it compile-time optional is free
  performance.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## d2fc3306 -- [process] Make current accessed only through its
   getter

Commit: d2fc33066f...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Sun Apr 15 23:44:59 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  +163 / -122 across 15 files. Every direct `current`
  deref in the kernel becomes a call to get_current_task()
  (and its process counterpart). process.h +6 net
  declares the getters. ~140 small per-file edits.

Context (guess -- from subject):

  Indirection through an accessor lets the getter evolve
  (add sanity checks, inline differently, become per-CPU
  when SMP arrives) without touching callers. Similar
  pattern to Linux's `current` macro.

## e92d803a -- [syscalls] Replace the incorrect -1 with -ENOSYS when
   not implemented

Commit: e92d803a60...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Mon Apr 16 18:42:13 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  +17 / -17 in arch/i386/syscalls.c. All "not implemented"
  stubs now return -ENOSYS rather than -1.

Context (guess -- from subject):

  -1 gives errno=EPERM (syscall 1 == exit, weirdness).
  -ENOSYS is the standard "this syscall isn't wired up"
  value and musl/glibc fallback paths recognize it.

## e6c54710 -- [set_thread_area] Use copy_from_user/copy_to_user

Commit: e6c5471090...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Mon Apr 16 19:00:10 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  +42 / -22 across gdt.c (+42 / -21) and syscalls.c (-1).
  set_thread_area's user_desc struct is now pulled via
  copy_from_user, filled in by the kernel, then pushed
  back with copy_to_user (the kernel rewrites the
  entry_number field when -1 on input).

Context (guess -- from subject):

  Harden the TLS-install syscall. Before this, a
  malicious user could fault mid-read and corrupt the
  GDT expansion.

## df9ec9a8 -- [sysenter] Make it work EXACTLY like the linux kernel

Commit: df9ec9a8b1...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Apr 17 19:54:59 2018 +0300

> Thanks to a special user-mapped page (like vsdo), now
> sysexit jumps there and, in usermode, restores ebp, edx,
> ecx and eip (with ret).

AI notes:

What changed:

  +57 / -6 across 4 files. entry.S +40 / -6: sysenter
  entry now arranges for sysexit to land in the vDSO
  trampoline. syscalls.c +3. elf.c +12: maps the
  trampoline page into every new process's address
  space. config.h +2 for the fixed user VA.

Context (from body):

  Minimal Linux-compatible sysenter path. Tilck gets a
  tiny one-page pseudo-vDSO that only holds the
  `pop/pop/pop/ret` epilogue Linux's vDSO exposes --
  enough for musl's sysenter stub to work unchanged.

  stack-layout reverse-engineering. Setting up a clean

## 4a6da78f -- [fault_resumable] Introduce fault_resumable(mask,
   func, nargs, ...)

Commit: 4a6da78f48...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Thu Apr 19 19:43:33 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  +222 / -9 across 7 files. New header
  exos/fault_resumable.h (+22) and impl (108 lines in
  arch/generic_x86/fault_resumable.c): a primitive to
  call a function while being prepared to recover from a
  selected set of CPU exceptions (mask). entry.S +34
  asm trampoline; idt.c +6 hooks; interrupts.c +13 / -2
  for recovery dispatch. testrunners.c +42 / -2 covers
  it.

Context (guess -- from subject):

  General-purpose fault-fixup primitive. Bigger brother
  of the copy_from_user fault-fixup: any kernel code can
  call fault_resumable(MASK_PAGE_FAULT, fn, ...) and
  get a clean error return if fn page-faults. Reusable
  for probing MMIO, kernel self-tests, etc.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## ef86dc02 -- [paging] Re-enable the page fault handler to run with
   IF = 1

Commit: ef86dc0216...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Apr 20 20:08:48 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  +29 / -9 across paging.c (+17 / -9) and interrupts.c
  (+12). Page-fault handler now runs with interrupts
  enabled -- the IRQ-critical sections inside are
  explicitly bracketed instead.

Context (guess -- from subject):

  Previously the PF handler ran with IF=0 (cli). That's
  safe but means no IRQs are serviced during faults,
  which hurts timer accuracy and responsiveness under
  heavy COW workloads.

## 106508fd -- [user] Remove the ASSERT(!is_preemption_enabled())
   limitation

Commit: 106508fd28...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Apr 20 21:00:22 2018 +0300

> Note: fixing the call-sites to call the *user*
> functions without disabling first the preemption.

AI notes:

What changed:

  +23 / -41 across 4 files. user.c -7 (ASSERT lifted).
  fs_syscalls.c -3 net, process.c -4 net, gdt.c -3 net
  remove their surrounding disable_preemption /
  enable_preemption pairs.

Context (from body):

  Old copy_from_user required preemption disabled
  because the fixup mechanism wasn't re-entrant. With
  fault_resumable being fully re-entrant (320c10fb),
  the limitation is gone -- callers can page-fault +
  yield during a user copy without kernel
  corruption.

## 5d116c94 -- [main] Make the kernel to support a custom command
   following the -cmd opt

Commit: 5d116c946f...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat May 5 23:50:59 2018 +0300

> When the kernel is booted with multiboot, it is
> possible to pass options through the 'cmdline'
> multiboot parameter. In case in the cmdline we have
> the option '-cmd', the arguments following will be
> treated as: <full path of an executable>
> [arg1 [arg2 [arg3 ...]]] This allows the kernel to
> execve() something else instead of /sbin/init, or to
> execve() init, but with some additional parameters.
> Now that init supports the '--' option allowing to
> forward the following arguments to the shell, with
> this patch it is possible to from the command line to
> forward arguments to our simple shell. This will be
> used by the system tests.

AI notes:

What changed:

  +33 / -4 across 4 files. parse_cmdline.c +27: -cmd
  option parsing. main.c +3 / -2 uses the parsed
  result to select init target + argv. kmalloc.c +2
  init touch. init.c 1-line.

Context (from body):

  Final piece: pass a full shell command through qemu
  -append -> multiboot cmdline -> kernel -> init --
  -> shell -c. System tests can drive tilck
  non-interactively.

## 3faef84c -- [tasklet] Make tasklets enqueue/run to run without
   disabling interrupts

Commit: 3faef84c66...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue May 8 13:33:01 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  +113 / -53 across 5 files. tasklet.c +52 net:
  lock-free enqueue/dequeue via atomic indices into
  the tasklet slot array. tasklet.h +5. se_tasklet.c
  +41 / -9 stress test. printk.c 1-line. run_all_tests
  +1.

Context (guess -- from subject):

  Tasklets are often enqueued from IRQ context; running
  the enqueue with interrupts disabled stretched
  IRQ-off windows. Lock-free ops mean IRQ handlers
  don't need to cli the tasklet queue.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 1bbd95e8 -- [tasklet][sched] Make tasklets to have absolute
   priority in the scheduler

Commit: 1bbd95e8d0...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue May 8 15:01:50 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  +33 / -5 across 3 files. tasklet.h +14 decls
  exposing a tasklet_runner_task pointer check.
  sched.c +9 net: if the tasklet runner is RUNNABLE,
  the scheduler picks it before any other task. tasklet.c
  +10 / -4 wakes the runner on enqueue.

Context (guess -- from subject):

  Tasklets are latency-critical (keyboard, timer-based
  wakeups); they should run as soon as possible,
  preempting user tasks.

## b8ec730d -- [timer] Make set_task_to_wake_after() work W/O
   disabling interrupts

Commit: b8ec730d98...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue May 8 15:24:18 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  +14 / -31 in timer_handler.c. Net -17. set_task_to_
  wake_after() loses its disable/enable_interrupts
  pairs and uses atomic ops on the timer list.

Context (guess -- from subject):

  Same motivation as 3faef84c for tasklets: shorter
  IRQ-off windows. Timer list is accessed both from
  the IRQ handler and from tasks; atomic ops let both
  co-exist.

## 750e0772 -- [ringbuf] Introduce ringbuf_unwrite()

Commit: 750e0772b9...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Wed May 9 15:48:15 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  +26 / -8 across ringbuf.h (+2) and ringbuf.c (+24
  / -6). New ringbuf_unwrite(n) rolls the write
  cursor back by n (useful for "speculatively wrote,
  abort").

Context (guess -- from subject):

  Tasklet enqueue needs this: it claims a slot, fills
  it, but must be able to rollback if a racing
  consumer drained in between.

## 365bbdcd -- [irq] Add debug logic to show amount of spurious IRQs

Commit: 365bbdcdfc...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Wed May 9 18:41:33 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  +14 / -4 across kb.c (+8) and irq.c (+6 / -4). IRQ
  handler tracks the count of spurious (ghost) IRQs
  per line; kb prints it when invoked by a command.

Context (guess -- from subject):

  Diagnostics for the 8259 PIC. Spurious IRQs happen
  when an interrupt clears before being acknowledged;
  a normal-but-low rate is expected, abnormally high
  suggests hardware/config trouble.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  quantum becomes HZ/20 (50 ms at 1000 Hz).

  rates.

## 60919400 -- [main] Don't use printk() before read_multiboot_info()

Commit: 609194008c...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat May 12 11:33:53 2018 +0300

> BONUS: entry: don't request VBE mode in the multiboot
> header

AI notes:

What changed:

  +16 / -16 across main.c (+12 / -10) and entry.S
  (+4 / -6). kmain() reorders startup so
  read_multiboot_info() runs before any printk(); the
  multiboot VBE mode flag is cleared.

Context (from body):

  printk needs to know the framebuffer/term config,
  which is set up from multiboot info. Calling it
  earlier either crashed or printed garbage.
  Dropping VBE lets the bootloader decide the mode.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 24339cf5 -- [efi bootloader] Use MS ABI and call UEFI funcs W/O
   the wrapper

Commit: 24339cf5e8...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Sun May 13 23:03:13 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  +82 / -124 across 4 files. Net -42.
  GenericBuild.cmake +2 adds -mabi=ms. efimain.c -68
  net drops the GNU-EFI uefi_call_wrapper indirection;
  direct function pointer calls with MS ABI.
  setup_graphics.c -24 net. utils.c small adjust.

Context (guess -- from subject):

  UEFI functions are MS ABI; GNU-EFI's
  uefi_call_wrapper shuffles args between ABIs.
  Compiling the bootloader with -mabi=ms removes
  that dance. Cleaner code + smaller binary.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## a3d24fd7 -- [efi bootloader] Fix a nasty bug: most of mbi may
   contained garbage

Commit: a3d24fd773...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Mon May 14 00:00:38 2018 +0300

> The trivial fix was just to bzero() it in
> efi_main().

AI notes:

What changed:

  +1 line in efimain.c: bzero(&mbi).

Context (from body):

  mbi was a local struct on the stack; unset fields
  held whatever was there before. Only the flags
  field was set; the kernel then interpreted
  uninitialised bytes as other tags. 1-line fix for
  a hours-of-debugging bug.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

> This change makes term interface functions to use
> action elems describing which action has to be
> performed and a term_execute_action() function,
> instead of directly performing any given action.
> This is clearly a preparation change. What will
> follow is a series of changes making the term
> interface functions to decide if any given action
> can be performed directly or has to be enqueued in
> a ring buffer, exactly as it happens in printk. The
> whole point is to make term run without disabling
> the interrupts and remain safely consistent. In
> case an interrupt handler calls a term function,
> interrupting a term function already running, its
> action will be enqueued and executed at the end of
> the original term function. Again, exactly like in
> the printk case.

AI notes:

What changed:

  +142 / -22 in term.c. A tagged-union "term action"
  struct (write char, scroll, set color, clear row,
  etc.) plus term_execute_action() dispatcher.
  term.h -1 (one public fn removed).

Context (from body):

  Apply the printk pattern to term: serialise all ops
  through one queue so re-entry is safe and no
  interrupts need masking.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## e427ef73 -- [uefi] Fixing all problems preventing kernel to work
   if booted with UEFI

Commit: e427ef73c8...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Mon May 14 23:37:01 2018 +0300

> Mostly, it was about using irq_mask_clear() for all
> IRQs and using a vaddr in kernel space for the
> framebuffer.

AI notes:

What changed:

  +11 / -6 across irq.c (+3 / -1) and fb_console.c
  (+8 / -5). IRQ mask reset at boot; FB base mapped
  into kernel VA space.

Context (from body):

  UEFI leaves the PIC masked differently from BIOS;
  explicit unmask. And the FB physical address is
  wherever UEFI placed it -- must be mapped in the
  kernel page tables before the console can touch
  it.

## 3e24d2dd -- [term][textmode_video] Impl. textmode_set_row()
   properly and use it in term

Commit: 3e24d2dd67...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue May 15 17:27:26 2018 +0300

> Result: the scroll consumes about 3 times less
> cycles

AI notes:

What changed:

  +2 / -8 across textmode_video.c and term.c.
  textmode_set_row uses memcpy for a whole row at
  once; term scroll takes advantage of it.

Context (from body):

  3x speedup. Per-char write was O(W*H) u16 stores;
  per-row is O(H) memcpy'd rows.

## 18d8e9be -- [fb_console] Optimize for the case of fonts with width
   = 8

Commit: 18d8e9bed6...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue May 15 19:30:08 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  +113 / -20 across 4 files. fb_raw.c +74 gains a
  dedicated fast path for 8-pixel-wide glyphs (one
  byte per glyph row -> unroll / SIMD-ish moves).
  fb_console.c / fb_int.h plumb the selection.
  vga_textmode_defs.h +3.

## 830d1835 -- [fb_console] Don't use (for the moment)
   fb_scroll_one_line_up()

Commit: 830d1835be...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Thu May 17 12:53:24 2018 +0300

> Reason: it appears to be 3x slower on bare-metal
> even if with QEMU it makes the 1-line scroll 2x
> faster.

AI notes:

What changed:

  +22 / -1 across term.c (+12) and fb_console.c
  (+10 / -1). The optimised path is kept but not
  used.

Context (from body):

  FB reads are expensive on real hardware (no cache
  on FB memory); QEMU's emulated FB is RAM-backed
  so it's fast. Trust the slower-but-faster-in-
  practice path until the write-combine issue can
  be addressed.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 18e38fa5 -- [fb_console] Introduce fb_flush() and
   fb_switch_to_shadow_buffer()

Commit: 18e38fa59e...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri May 18 12:21:23 2018 +0300

> Note: the functionality is currently disabled.

AI notes:

What changed:

  +64 / -19 across 7 files. fb_raw.c +29 net,
  fb_console.c -4 net, term.c +15 (flush plumbing).
  fb_flush copies from a shadow RAM buffer to the
  real FB; shadow buffer lives in normal cached
  memory so writes are fast.

Context (from body):

  Addresses the "FB writes are slow on real
  hardware" problem noted in 830d1835. Shadow buffer
  + batched flush can massively speed things up.
  Marked "disabled" because not yet correct in all
  paths.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 6a9a09ef -- [printk] Fix bug in vsnprintk()

  the formatting buffer.

Context (from body):

  Textbook mistake: "print char" path bypassed the
  buffer. Easy fix, but would have caused garbled
  output or double-prints.

## ade76970 -- [efi bootloader] Load only the used part of the FAT32
   ramdisk

Commit: ade769702b...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri May 18 19:03:30 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  +38 / -9 across efimain.c (+33 / -9) and
  fat32_base.h (+5). Bootloader reads the FAT
  header to learn used-byte count, then loads only
  that many bytes rather than the whole image.

Context (guess -- from subject):

  Boot-time speedup: loading a 10 MB ramdisk when
  only 3 MB are used is waste.

## 37fc1816 -- [self_tests] Fix fault_resumable_perf

Commit: 37fc18161a...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat May 19 17:53:35 2018 +0300

> The bug was the call to enable_preemption() hitting
> the oldval > 0 assert. This happened because now
> self tests are run always in a dedicated kernel
> thread and preemption is enabled by default.
> Therefore, as expected, enable_preemption()
> complained about that. The test has just not been
> updated since the change. The fix simply removes
> the now useless calls to enable/disable
> preemption.

  +4 / -8 in se_fault_resumable.c.

Context (from body):

  Test was written before 0316e0f2 (selftests run
  in dedicated kthread). With preemption already
  enabled in the test's environment, toggling it
  trips the counter ASSERT.

## 49b36690 -- [bootloader][main] Use multiboot and make the kernel
   to depend on that

Commit: 49b36690b4...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat May 19 20:00:18 2018 +0300

> This change makes the legacy bootloader to use
> the multiboot v1 protocol to boot the kernel and,
> at the same time, at the kernel side any support
> for non-multiboot is removed.
>
> BONUS: term can be initialized also in panic
> scenarios and even if kmalloc does not work,
> thanks to a BSS failsafe buffer.

AI notes:

What changed:

  +77 / -23 across 8 files. stage3.c +46 / -1
  emits multiboot v1 header + info. main.c -12 net
  drops the legacy path. term.c +18 / -4 gains a
  BSS failsafe buffer for panic-time init.
  kmalloc.h +2, misc.c +2 / -2, config.h -5,
  boot.S +3, efimain.c +2.

Context (from body):

  Both bootloaders now speak the same multiboot
  dialect to the kernel. One less code path to
  maintain. Bonus hardens the panic output.

## 5ec7228b -- [bootloader] Use unreal mode in call_realmode_func()

Commit: 5ec7228b60...

Date:   Mon May 21 12:29:08 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  +26 / -15 across stage3.c (+1 / -1) and
  stage3_entry.S (+25 / -14). The code enters
  "unreal mode" (16-bit CS but 32-bit data segment
  limits) to avoid the 64KB data-segment cap.

Context (guess -- from subject):

  Classic trick: BIOS calls need real-mode CS, but
  the C code around them wants full 4GB addressing.
  Unreal mode gives both.

## 5bac5e63 -- [bootloader] Set SS to BASE_SEG and make gdt16 entry
   0x08 to use BASE_SEG

Commit: 5bac5e633d...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Mon May 21 17:46:43 2018 +0300

> This saves us from copying the 16-bit code to low
> addresses (< 64 KB).

What changed:

  +26 / -40 across stage3.c (-8) and
  stage3_entry.S (+26 / -32). Net -14 lines.

Context (from body):

  Previously the 16-bit real-mode code had to live
  at paddr < 64KB so far segments could address it.
  With SS/CS based at BASE_SEG where stage3 already
  lives, no copy is needed.

> in:
>
>     vbe_set_video_mode(u8 mode)
>
> Therefore, to completely fix the problem it was
> enough to just use u16. Now, the graphics console
> works when bpp = 32 (the support for bpp = 24 has
> not been implemented yet).

  removed). vbe.c/h 1-line each: u8 -> u16 for the
  mode number.

  Days-of-debugging bug. VBE mode numbers are 16
  bits but the signature used u8; modes >= 0x100
  truncated to 0. Fix is one keyword. Tiny cause,
  big symptom.

## f83bc820 -- [bootloader] Move the loading of the RAMDISK in stage3

Commit: f83bc820ba...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Thu May 24 18:51:28 2018 +0300

> NOTE: the correctness of the new load ramdisk
> code has been checked with a CRC32 calculated on
> the first 33 MB of the fatpart.

AI notes:

What changed:

  +193 / -186 across 5 files. boot.S -186 net:
  sheds the ramdisk load (which was done in stage1
  via int 13h). stage3.c +10 net invokes the new
  path. stage3_rm_calls.S +158 impl. realmode_call.c
  +13.

Context (from body):

  Big architectural move: stage1 (boot.S) only
  needs to load stage3 now, not the whole
  ramdisk. stage3 (C code) reads the ramdisk via
  its own BIOS-call mechanism. More flexible -- can
  do dynamic sizing, error handling. CRC32 check
  confirms correctness.

## 286d561a -- [bootloader][boot.S] Avoid copying complete_flush to
   a low addr

Commit: 286d561aa9...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat May 26 13:55:51 2018 +0300

> How: by using a GDT with code and data at offset
> (DEST_DATA_SEGMENT*16), instead of using a "flat
> GDT".

AI notes:

What changed:

  +47 / -69 across boot.S (-22 net) and stage3.c
  (-1).

Context (from body):

  Non-flat GDT lets far jump resolve to an address
  in the already-loaded stage1 page, without copying
  the flush routine to low memory first. 22 fewer
  lines in the cramped MBR.

## 9caada8c -- [bootloader] Fix a nasty bug preventing some BIOS
   calls to work

Commit: 9caada8c7a...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat May 26 21:16:01 2018 +0300

> All the segment registers (except CS) were left
> untouched (unreal mode). That was causing some
> BIOS code to execute an invalid instruction (on 2
> different machines). The solution was to leave
> just 'fs' for unreal mode and properly set the
> others.

AI notes:

What changed:

  +10 / -4 in stage3_entry.S. DS/ES/SS reset to
  16-bit values before BIOS call; only FS remains
  in unreal mode for C-side data access.

Context (from body):

  Running on real hardware exposed this: some
  BIOSes assume 16-bit DS/ES and crash if they
  find 32-bit limits. Fix works on 2 real
  machines.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 3d86b217 -- [bootloader] Remove the flat binary stage3 and use
   only elf_stage3

Commit: 3d86b217e6...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat May 26 22:19:43 2018 +0300

> By just skipping the first 4 KB, it is possible
> for the asm bootloader to jump into an ELF
> binary, as if it was a flat binary, IF and ONLY
> IF, for each section, the file offset and the
> vaddr differ always by the same, constant,
> offset. That's the case for elf_stage3. Using an
> ELF binary allows correct debugging in any case.
> The only price to pay here for using an ELF
> binary is that we'll never be able to embed the
> early boot code into the binary. That could
> happen only if a flat binary is used. But that
> is too bad for debugging. Creating a parallel
> ELF binary of a flat binary just for debugging
> does not completely work because addresses in
> .data and in .bss differ.

AI notes:

What changed:

  +58 / -78 across 8 files. Net -20.
  common_linker_script.ld removed. elf_stage3_script.
  ld expanded. bootloader/CMakeLists.txt -22 net.
  Flat-binary target gone.

Context (from body):

  Slick trick: a carefully-laid-out ELF binary
  satisfies the bootloader's expectations (jump to
  offset 0x1000 == entry point) while also being
  a proper debuggable ELF. One artifact for both.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Date:   Sun May 27 21:57:54 2018 +0300

> This makes the code more elegant and simpler at
> the same time.

  is pre-populated in .data; no runtime patch.

Context (from body):

  in is always cleaner than computing it at boot.

> fault_resumable_call(): now they work smoothly.
> Also, panic() will initialize term in case it
> has not been initialized yet.

  __current set very early. process32.c -11 (old
  delayed init removed). process.h +1. entry.S,
  debug_utils.c, term.c, main.c small.

Context (from body):

  (see ee506509). Now the invariant holds from
  kmain's first line, enabling panic / copy /
  fault_resumable very early.

  faster.

## 1d7205cf -- [fpu_memcpy] Optimize fpu_memcpy_single_256_nt() by
   using hot-patching

Commit: 1d7205cf3f...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Wed May 30 19:59:29 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  +70 / -35 across 6 files. fpu_memcpy.c +42 net
  implements runtime code patching: the dispatch
  site is overwritten with a jmp to the chosen
  implementation once at feature-detect time.
  entry.S +11 helpers. elf.c +7 / -2 handles the
  mapped text for patching.

Context (guess -- from subject):

  "Alternatives" technique from Linux: after
  choosing the best impl, burn it into the call
  site so the dispatch becomes free.

## 2c3cb269 -- [fb_console] Use double buffering only when running on
   bare-metal

Commit: 2c3cb269bc...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Wed May 30 21:13:50 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  +21 / -3 across 4 files. hal.h +4 bare-metal
  detection. fb_console.c +7 / -1 disables shadow
  buffer under qemu. cpu_features.h +5. main.c
  +5 / -2.

Context (guess -- from subject):

  Shadow buffer helps on real hardware (slow FB
  writes) but adds memcpy overhead in qemu where
  FB is just RAM. Detect hypervisor via CPUID and
  skip shadow when detected.

## 7ef72bd6 -- [fb_console][panic] Support panic at the earliest
   possible

Commit: 7ef72bd65f...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Wed May 30 22:19:34 2018 +0300

> console, to see a panic message very early:
> immediately after read_multiboot_info(), where
> we get the address of the framebuffer. In text
> mode it's possible instead to see a panic even
> before the first call in kmain() because the
> address of the character framebuffer is fixed.

  bootstrap path that can write to FB using only
  identity-mapped physical address (no paging
  needed). paging_int.h +7 / 0 decls. Other small
  tweaks.

Context (from body):

  Panics during early boot are now visible. Huge
  usability win when chasing boot-time bugs.

Context (from body):

  After extensive measurement, the shadow-buffer
  approach isn't uniformly a win. A long comment
  (referenced by the subject) documents the
  trade-offs. Default OFF; can be enabled where
  proven beneficial.

## 209f46a7 -- [fb_console] Performance improvements

Commit: 209f46a7c5...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 1 14:02:24 2018 +0300

> Restore the double buffering but add a 'flush'
> parameter to vi->set_row(): it allows term to
> use it when scrolling. In fb_console, the text
> in first drawn on the shadow buffer and
> immediately (if flush is true) flushed. This
> approach is ultimately much faster on real
> hardware, compared to the draw line-by-line and
> a big final flush, because of the time-locality.
> The scroll_one_line_up() is about 13% faster.

AI notes:

What changed:

  +27 / -79 across 5 files. Net -52. fb_console.c
  +13 / -28, fb_raw.c -53 net. video_interface
  `set_row(..., flush)` gains the parameter;
  textmode passes false, fb uses it.

Context (from body):

  Double buffering is re-enabled with a smarter
  policy: flush per row (while the row is still
  in cache), not at the end. 13% scroll speedup
  on real HW.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## aa8cd844 -- [build][cmake] Introduce the CMake function hex2dec()

Commit: aa8cd844a9...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 1 22:14:38 2018 +0300

> Unfortunately, CMake's MATH function does not
> understand hex numbers, while they are often
> pretty useful, especially in linker scripts.
> Therefore, adding a pure-CMake implementation of
> hex2dec().

AI notes:

What changed:

  +66 / -10 across CMakeLists.txt (+62 / -6) and
  gen_config.cmake (+4 / -3). Pure-CMake parser
  for hex strings.

Context (from body):

  Addresses are naturally hex (0x20000). Prior to
  this, CMake couldn't do arithmetic on them, so
  constants had to be dec in CMake and hex in C.
  hex2dec removes that split.

## 3676ffef -- [build][build_toolchain] SWITCH to a LIBMUSL gcc
   toolchain

Commit: 3676ffeff0...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 8 12:21:35 2018 +0300

> This major-impact change makes the whole project
> to use a musl-based i686 toolchain instead of a
> "generic" glibc one + many compiler & linker
> options necessary to build & link with dietlibc.
> Libmusl is by far more supported as a lightweight
> "libc" than dietlibc: major projects like
> "busybox" can be built with it. Therefore, the
> #1 reason for switching to this library is being
> able to compile busybox and other open source
> projects. The #2 reason is using a pre-built gcc
> toolchain configured to work with libmusl by
> default: this makes the build of exOS's userapps
> simpler but also allows any project potentially
> to be built with that toolchain. With dietlibc
> that is by far harder and, given the fact that
> the library is not supported anymore, does not
> make much sense to invest any effort in it.
>
> However, it worth to mention that dietlibc
> helped A LOT in the development of exOS, because
> of its very limited requirements towards the
> kernel. Supporting libmusl it's possible only
> today after implementing (partially or
> completely) many "newer" syscalls, that were
> necessary even for a program to reach main().

AI notes:

What changed:

  +17 / -106 across 9 files. Net -89. scripts/
  build_toolchain -53 (dietlibc build logic gone).
  build_fatpart -12 (the dual-build hack from
  b9e41de5). musl-gcc wrapper removed. Per-file
  dietlibc flags deleted from every CMakeLists.
  usermode_apps/CMakeLists.txt -28.

Context (from body):

  Milestone commit. dietlibc served well in the
  early kernel days but musl is actively
  maintained and compatible with real-world
  software. After this commit, everything builds
  against musl by default.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 9bac6474 -- [build_toolchain] Introduce the [non-default]
   build_busybox command

Commit: 9bac647445...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 8 15:22:36 2018 +0300

Commit: a947e2e0c5...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Jun 12 22:28:14 2018 +0300

> The function was indeed doing correctly its job
> in coalescing as much as possible nodes above
> the just-freed one BUT, after doing that it did
> NOT mark as NOT FULL the nodes above the last
> coalesced one. Even if they cannot be coalesced,
> because they are split and half of them is not
> empty, still they are clearly NOT FULL. This is
> a particularly corner case that was not handled
> before.

  (+8 / -4), unit test (-3 net).

Context (from body):

  partial free. Allocations for that size would
  skip the now-available slot. A "silent slow
  leak" bug.

## 38e19932 -- Merge branch 'master' of github.com/vvaltchev/
   experimentOs [6 commits: build_toolchain modularisation]

Commit: 38e199321e...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Mon Jun 18 10:29:21 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  Merge commit. Aggregate diff: +585 / -509
  across 9 files, net +76. Files touched:
  scripts/build_toolchain (-467), scripts/cmake_run
  (-30), scripts/script_utils (+71 new),
  scripts/tc/install_pkgs (+257 new),
  scripts/tc/cmake_download (+78 new),
  scripts/tc/gcc_tc (+91 new), plus three
  qemu launch scripts gaining a few lines each.

Context (from merge shape -- generic "Merge
branch 'master' of ..." subject):

  This is a GitHub-flow pull: locally-developed
  commits merged with remotely-pushed master.
  The 6 branch commits are all build-toolchain
  refactor work done Sat Jun 16 2018.

  The work is a modularisation of the monolithic
  scripts/build_toolchain into a scripts/tc/
  subdir with dedicated files per concern:

  - 431fa738 "Minor improvements" -- 3 qemu
    scripts gain +7-8 lines each; build_toolchain
    +19 net misc tweaks.

  - 60621ec1 "Refactoring" -- the BIG one.
    scripts/build_toolchain shrinks by 295 net
    lines. New scripts/tc/install_pkgs (+253)
    holds the distro-specific apt/yum/pacman
    logic previously inline. scripts/script_utils
    (+26) holds shared bash helpers.
    scripts/cmake_run (-21 net) also trimmed.

  - 2489fc14 "Extract gcc_tc and cmake_downloads"
    -- next modularisation step. Two more files
    peel out: scripts/tc/cmake_download (+76)
    handles the CMake tarball fetch, scripts/tc/
    gcc_tc (+95) handles the GCC toolchain
    install. build_toolchain loses another 169
    net lines.

  - dac659b6 "Introduce show_work_on_component_
    msg" -- adds a consistent status-line helper
    to script_utils; each extracted sub-script
    uses it, saving -7 net lines overall.

  - 58cd10e5 "Fix a bug in cmake_download" --
    +25 / -12. Bug in the just-extracted
    cmake_download helper (likely a path or
    retry issue).

  - 137a4dc6 "Improve cmake_run (refactoring)" --
    cmake_run -7 net, script_utils +26 (shared
    CMake invocation helpers).

  Net outcome: the old ~500-line
  build_toolchain shell script becomes a small
  dispatcher that sources helpers from
  scripts/tc/ and scripts/script_utils.
  scripts/tc/ is the germ of what later becomes
  scripts/pkgmgr/ (mentioned in CLAUDE.md as the
  Ruby pkgmgr's predecessor on master).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 20815d95 -- [exos] Don't use -nostdinc anymore

Commit: 20815d9567...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Jun 19 13:42:01 2018 +0300

> After implementing "many" syscalls, it turned
> out that it gets harder and harder to keep the
> project completely isolated from the system
> headers. In particular, the following problem
> turned out to be critical: the noarch part of
> the kernel needs to build for x86_64 (for the
> unit tests) and, at the same time, it needs to
> put data structures like "struct stat" in
> common headers. Those common exos headers get
> included indirectly by the unit tests and
> clearly the exos definitions conflict with the
> system ones, visible by the unit tests. In
> particular, the definitions, even if isolated
> (unit tests to never include exos headers)
> they would be based on the assumptions that
> the system is 32-bit (because of the length of
> some fields). That may cause nasty and
> hard-to-debug issues. Therefore, at this point
> using linux's system headers seems more
> convenient because:
>
>     - Saves exOS from copying (carefully!) a
>       lot of stuff from the system headers
>       (less code, less errors).
>
>     - Makes automatically exOS to use the
>       right data structures, when the arch
>       changes.
>
> Clearly, there are still issues with this
> approach as well. For example, linux supports
> the same data structure in different sizes,
> depending on the syscall (sys_stat64 vs
> sys_oldstat). In that case, in order to
> implement sys_oldstat(), exOS will still need
> to have a dedicated "struct oldstat" which
> differs from what usermode apps compiled with
> the exOS toolchain (modern gcc + libmusl)
> see as "struct stat". But that still seems
> acceptable, considering that, for a pretty
> long time exOS will run only programs
> compiled with its toolchain. And, even when
> exOS will have to support "struct oldstat",
> it still would mean to have just 1 copied
> struct instead of 2 (the modern one is given
> by the system headers).

AI notes:

What changed:

  +21 / -127 across 10 files. Net -106.
  -nostdinc dropped from all CMakeLists.
  system_headers/stdint.h -7 (no longer shim).
  sys_types.h -69 (defs replaced by system
  headers). basic_defs.h -32 net. Multiple .c
  files +/- adjust types.

Context (from body):

  Philosophical shift after Tilck matures past
  kernel-only. Use Linux's uapi headers as
  type source of truth; copy only the few
  structs that actually differ. Excellent
  commit message — classic Vlad style, written
  as a design decision log.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 8b5e06cb -- [exvfs] Change the interface of several exvfs_* funcs

Commit: 8b5e06cbd0...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Thu Jun 21 21:25:43 2018 +0300

> This change makes exvfs funcs to never return
> directly a handle, but to use an out-parameter
> instead. This allows the use of return codes
> for handling several errors, typically -ENOMEM.
> A particularly good example is open(): it could
> fail because of the file does not exist or
> because there is no enough memory to allocate
> the file handle.

AI notes:

What changed:

  +88 / -72 across 10 files. Net +16.
  exvfs_open/create/... all switch from `handle
  f()` to `int f(..., handle *out)`. All callers
  updated.

Context (from body):

  Standard Unix idiom: return -errno as int, pass
  result via out-param. Lets the FS layer
  distinguish "no such file" (-ENOENT) from "out
  of memory" (-ENOMEM).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 7e7e8883 -- [exvfs] Fix a bug in check_mountpoint_match() and
   exvfs_open()

Commit: 7e7e88836a...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Jun 26 14:42:05 2018 +0300

> The code was matching a path like "/dev" to "/",
> even when a mountpoint "/dev/" existed. That's
> because mountpoints always end with "/", while
> paths never do. Handling for this special case
> has been added. Now, if a mountpoint "/xxx/"
> exists, the path directory "/xxx" refers to the
> root ("/") of filesystem mounted at "xxx"
> instead of the directory "xxx" rooted at "/".
>
> BONUS: find has been added in busybox.

AI notes:

What changed:

  +55 / -42 across 6 files. exvfs.c +15 / -10 new
  prefix-match logic. Tests +12 / -6.
  busybox.config 24 lines flipped (find
  enabled).

Context (from body):

  Mountpoint boundary bug. After fix, `ls /dev`
  shows the devfs contents not /dev as a
  filesystem entry.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 34b42750 -- [exos] Re-org the include/ directory

Commit: 34b4275024...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Wed Jul 4 20:55:59 2018 +0300

>    - Move include/common/ => include/exos/common
>    - Move include/exos/ => include/exos/kernel
>
> This way, everything related to exOS is under
> include/exos/. The change is huge because of
> the fixes in all the source files and headers.

AI notes:

What changed:

  +595 / -595 across 163 files -- perfectly
  balanced (pure move pattern). Every .c/.h/.S
  updates its #includes from <common/...> /
  <exos/...> to <exos/common/...> /
  <exos/kernel/...>.

Context (from body):

  Second major header reorganisation (first was
  the 13-step pass around commit ~610). Result:
  a single include root (include/exos/) that
  the -I path points at. Every internal header
  lives under it, namespaced by kernel/common.
  This is the include layout referenced in
  CLAUDE.md today (with "exos" -> "tilck"
  rename yet to come).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 6f6f85ea -- [elf_loader] Fix a bug in the ELF loader

Commit: 6f6f85ea37...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Thu Jul 12 02:29:15 2018 +0300

> In case two LOAD phdr overlap on the same page
> and that page is RO, we'll get a PAGE FAULT
> with the previous implementation because
> load_phdr() was setting pages to RO (when
> necessary) at its end (so the next load_phdr(),
> if a page overlaps, generates a fault). The
> simple fix is to do the access setting only
> after loading all the phdrs.

AI notes:

What changed:

  +30 / -3 in elf.c. Defer RO marking to after
  all LOADs.

Context (from body):

  Real-world ELFs (especially statically-linked
  BusyBox) have LOAD segments that share a page.
  Marking RO eagerly broke the next LOAD.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## b35ccd2d -- [interrupts] DEBUG_check_preemption_enabled_for_
   usermode only for syscalls

Commit: b35ccd2dec...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Jul 14 19:22:15 2018 +0300

> After making possible IRQ handlers to ask for
> a thread to be immediately scheduled, we
> created a new state: no preemption + 0 nested
> interrupts. When an IRQ comes in this state,
> the check_preemption_enabled_for_usermode
> called by irq_entry() fails causing a kernel
> panic. A good enough solution is just to run
> the check for syscalls. Afterall, it is only
> a debug check useful to prevent buggy kernel
> changes that allow returning to usermode with
> preemption disabled: it does not need to run
> all the time. Check that at each syscall is
> good enough.

AI notes:

What changed:

  +6 / -5 in interrupts.c.

Context (from body):

  Regression from 0c0c07c4. Gate the assertion
  appropriately.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 1601-1625 kmalloc API refactor arc
   (fce33e4c..210d5af7, Aug 3-7 2018)

Rather than listing 25 small commits separately, this range is one
coherent refactoring of the kmalloc allocator. Highlights:

  - fce33e4c / 4d06df92 / 0cb51c98: extract internal_kmalloc_aux(),
    introduce calculate_node_size(), make `size` an in/out param so
    the bottom-most allocator reports exact usage back up.

  - 8c40157c / d7117d3e: implement and test
    internal_kmalloc_split_block() -- split a bigger block into the
    requested size + a residue block.

  - 61b470a5: implement internal_kmalloc_coalesce_block() (merge
    adjacent free blocks).

  - 35f3d9a1: add do_actual_alloc param -- lets higher layers ask
    "what would happen?" without committing.

  - bcc449b5 / 3ce0ed30: rename internal_kmalloc -> per_heap_kmalloc,
    interal_kfree2 -> per_heap_kfree.

  - 665e67bc: add multi_step_alloc param -- one allocation may span
    multiple sub-blocks (needed by mmap).

  - f606bca5 / 0bcf58b9: tests added / rewritten.

  - 90ac5580 / 944aee48: add sub_blocks_min_size param and
    corresponding multi_step_free; 2bad6c56 switches mmap/munmap to
    use them.

  - f0e60889 / b12c91ca / c1e34272: measure oom_test performance,
    polish, raise test severity.

  - dd3598b5 / 65d456a8 / 5bfee32f / 4cf1177e / 00b39843: final
    shape -- kmalloc/kfree2 become thin wrappers over
    general_kmalloc/general_kfree (new general_kmalloc.c.h), which
    dispatch to the correct heap's per_heap_* primitives. In/out
    size param propagates all the way down.

  - 705797ea: mmap() calls bzero() on new allocations.

  - 210d5af7: user_valloc_and_map() is optimised -- fewer page
    walks for contiguous allocations.

Total diff: rough order of ~+1.2k / -0.8k across kmalloc.c,
kmalloc_heaps.c.h, kmalloc_block_node.h, general_kmalloc.c.h,
process_mm.c, devshell_commands.c, unittests/kmalloc_test.cpp, and
kmalloc.h. Net result: a cleaner three-layer API
(general -> per-heap -> block primitives), with proper unit test
coverage for split/coalesce/multi-step paths.

Context (guess):

  Motivation is preparing kmalloc to back a real mmap/munmap --
  variable-sized user allocations that must be tracked precisely
  for munmap to undo them.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 1651-1670 kmalloc: mdalloc + small-heap arc
   (e198718d..f011ddf5, Aug 15-19 2018)

Second wave of kmalloc refactoring. Individually small commits,
collectively introduce:

  - e198718d: micro-opt inside internal_kmalloc (10-15 cycles).

  - 9f4a717a: NEW mdalloc() / mdfree() public API that stores the
    allocation size in a hidden metadata header, so the caller can
    free without remembering the size (malloc-style convenience
    over the in/out-size primitives).

  - 57b52397 / 7ce1e0b4 / ed4d0264 / f569da76: migrate gdt,
    elf_loader, fs/mp.c, and FPU-state alloc from kfree() to
    kfree2() / mdalloc.

  - 67b2e157: mdalloc header gains a magic field (debug builds).

  - 1bbdae56: ext_string_util adopts mdalloc.

  - f39e1f8e: REMOVE old kfree(void *ptr) -- all callers have
    migrated.

  - de5029cc: per_heap_kfree(NULL) is a no-op (Linux-style).

  - 55eca3b5: general_kfree may assume size != 0.

  - 33624120 / 9c18ce48: small-heap kmalloc -- a separate
    sub-allocator for tiny allocations backed by a pool of 16-byte
    aligned buddy slots (fixes the 'fpu' test's alignment
    requirement).

  - 9cd29496 / 259fbc6c: refactor + add aligned-alloc support to
    small heaps.

  - 1ab2a064: aligned_kmalloc() API + align type baked into flags.

  - 916af1df: split off kmalloc_small_heap.c.h (+199 new,
    general_kmalloc.c.h -179).

  - 9a7dabcc: small-heaps stats hook.

  - de9af241 / f011ddf5: typo + README.md update (20 line doc add).

Net effect: kmalloc now has a two-tier architecture (normal heap +
small heap), a malloc-style mdalloc wrapper, and uniform flag-based
API. Shape is much closer to what Tilck ships today.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 0d7853f2 -- [process][sched] Fix a nasty bug related with the
   size of task_info

Commit: 0d7853f261...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Mon Aug 20 14:58:02 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  sched.c +34 / -26 net. Bug: the formula
  `ti = ((task_info *)pi) - 1` failed in release builds when the
  compiler inserted padding between two separately declared
  `static` structs. Fix: allocate one byte buffer sized as the
  concatenation of both structs and compute offsets explicitly.

  placing two independent static vars adjacently".

## 1690-1706 build/gtest/mmap bug-hunt arc
   (4319169d..3e646035, Aug 23-26 2018)

Block of CI/test plumbing + one "scary" 32/64-bit bug:

  - 4319169d / 14e64982 / dae53744 / 762baf70: googletest built
    out-of-tree in the toolchain; new build_gtest_arch helper
    and ARCH_GTESTS CMake option lets gtest be cross-built for
    the target arch and run in the kernel via a dedicated
    self-test entrypoint.

  - 1ed9327d / 0093babf / 3032a180: new unittests/mem_regions.cpp
    unit test that exercises system_mmap edge cases; reduced to
    a minimal reproducer.

  - 4230f1c1: *fix for the reproduced bug*. In
    align_mem_regions_to_page_boundary() a u64 addr was AND'ed
    with a 32-bit PAGE_MASK constant, truncating when
    addr >= 4 GiB. Fix: 64-bit mask.

  - 3f0eacb1 / 9db0ea0f / 54bfa322 / 5ad4d608 / 28d43df2 /
    7226c8f1 / 3e646035: CircleCI config tweaks, test renames,
    new fixture style, add debug_arch_gtests_build job.

Net: ~+300 / -160 across the build system and tests; plus one
latent 32/64-bit bug squashed.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 1707-1715 MTRR, PAT and framebuffer WC mapping arc
   (a11b2503..d22e5982, Aug 23-28 2018)

Proper memory-type management for the linear framebuffer:

  - a11b2503: fb_raw maps the framebuffer with an MTRR set to
    write-combining (was just WT+CD before). Major perf
    improvement for large-area writes.

  - b06d4c6b / a07ad87a: extract the fb-in-kernel-space mapping
    into paging.c; move MTRR code into its own mtrr.c (-95 lines
    out of fb_raw.c / paging.c).

  - 8ee04a2f / 27a611e6 / 9a0a3d58: new dump_var_mtrrs() debug
    helper + PAT (Page Attribute Table) definitions + begin PAT
    support in paging.c (+68 / -15 net).

  - 359bd794: drop pitch_size_buf (dead helper).

  - e133c830: *first Tilck-specific syscall*: sys_tilck_cmd --
    umbrella syscall used by devshell / selftests to issue
    privileged kernel-test operations.

  - d22e5982: devshell commands gain argc/argv.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 2f8b1db8 -- [mtrr] Introduce pre/post_mttr_change()

Commit: 2f8b1db8a9...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Aug 31 19:30:09 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  mtrr.c +100 / -2, x86_utils.h +49. Proper SDM-style MTRR-change
  sequence: disable caches, flush, invalidate TLBs, update, then
  re-enable.

Context:

  Required before runtime MTRR changes (e.g. setting WC on the
  framebuffer region) are safe on all CPUs.

## b739bea8 -- [sched] Make task_change_state() to run with IF = 0

Commit: b739bea8bd...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Sep 1 14:59:20 2018 +0300

AI notes:

What changed:

  process.c -21 net, sched.c +10 net, timer_handler.c +3.
  task_change_state now requires interrupts-off at entry;
  callers handle the save/restore.

Context:

  Prevents an IRQ arriving in the middle of state transition and
  observing an inconsistent tasklist.

## 1728-1742 fb_console + PAT + fpu_memset256 arc
   (27e2fc65..bbcec725, Sep 1-10 2018)

Graphics-console polish and a performance breakthrough:

  - 27e2fc65 / 2e4128777d / a11b6d64 / b11d5a6a / f23bfd01: MTRR
    refactor, fb_perf self-tests, and *fpu_memset256* -- a
    256-bit SIMD memset used to clear the framebuffer.

  - 8b3b05ac: use PAT to mark the framebuffer as WC.

  - 545afb39: **remove the shadow buffer**. After marking the
    framebuffer WC via PAT, raw writes got ~200x faster, so the
    double-buffer is now a pessimisation. fb_console.c -107 net.

  - bfe7f5fb: kcond disables IRQs around state transitions
    (companion to the earlier sched.c work).

  - adf9f109: mtrr cosmetic.

  - ed9a52a9 / 36511381: termtest app with console_perf_test()
    + standalone-buildable.

  - 9c07af25: fix bootloader RAMDISK_PADDR constant.

  - f289ebf1 / bbcec725: multi-colour-mode support propagated
    from the bootloader to fb_console (RGB8, RGBX8, indexed
    BIOS modes).

  - 05054469: new usermode_apps/fbtest.c demo app.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 1743-1754 /dev/fb0 + mmap-of-device-files arc
   (cca83281..2247920, Sep 10-14 2018)

Real framebuffer access from user space lands:

  - cca83281: fix sys_close() bug.

  - ece73e63: new kernel/char/fbdev.c (+48); registers /dev/fb0.

  - e70c649b / 9c982f64 / 4ce3e69c / c4c4fee4: sys_mmap_pgoff()
    learns to mmap *device* files (not just anon pages).
    KMALLOC_FL_NO_ACTUAL_ALLOC lets mmap reserve a VA range
    without backing RAM.

  - 64c37236: fbtest app exercises /dev/fb0.

  - adafab4b: FBIOGET_FSCREENINFO / FBIOGET_VSCREENINFO ioctls.

  - 4b01ac35: actual mmap of fb0 to user (the previous pieces
    were plumbing; this commits the end-to-end path).

  - 4df0d47f / 52e433a7: per-process user-mapping tracker (so
    munmap / process exit can tear them down);
    redraw_static_elements + partial KDSETMODE(KD_TEXT).

  - 2247920: complete KDSETMODE(KD_GRAPHICS) -- term.c +63 adds
    pause/restart video output.

Net: user-space apps can now mmap /dev/fb0 and switch the tty
into graphics mode (like Linux VT_SETMODE + /dev/fbN).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 1787-1800 USE_SYSCC + musl wrapper + build cross-arch arc
   (ae6849b6..2163e751, Oct 13-20 2018)

Major build-system rework:

  - ae6849b6: **USE_SYSCC=1** mode -- build the kernel with the
    host ($CC) compiler instead of the cross-compiler. Touches
    CMake scaffolding (+143/-33 across 16 files), basic_defs.h,
    sync.h (restricts GCC-specific builtins behind feature
    probes), and the fb_raw / fpu_memcpy / timer_handler tweaks
    needed to compile with stricter host CCs.

  - 3c1c3de4: extract utils into other/utils.cmake.

  - 2be54d91 / 8f58e0fa: new set_cross_compiler() and
    set_cross_compiler_glibc() helpers (replace copy-paste in
    per-subdir CMakeLists).

  - Merge commits 1791 (38816fe6) + 1792 (5d95f1eb): "readme"
    branch merged. Introduces 339-line README rewrite + tetris.png
    screenshot (7 branch commits 20d49b53..7b79966a were
    README.md revisions only).

  - 7b0dc015: build_fatpart wasn't copying the EFI boot file --
    fix.

  - bf5634da / ee9f22b9 / 5b5dde09: host arch must be x86_64 (hard
    fail otherwise); OBJCOPY / STRIP env vars; new ARCH_GCC_TC
    var that packs arch + GCC version + toolchain prefix into one
    thing.

  - c87a190d / 87d5b830 / e2a2bb96: small polish; **musl-g++
    wrapper** introduced (other/musl-g++) alongside musl-gcc --
    enables building C++ usermode apps against musl. utils.cmake
    +89.

  - 2163e751: new `TARGET_ARCH` env var caches as CMake `${ARCH}`.

Net: the build system now supports both a cross-compiled kernel
(classic) and a host-compiled kernel (USE_SYSCC); user apps can
be C or C++ via musl; the kernel no longer hardcodes any single
toolchain.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 1801-1813 USE_SYSCC polish + boot tree reorg
   (ab351f36..e7d31981, Oct 30-Dec 13 2018)

  - ab351f36 / 6324c7d4 / f07c4ee4: i386-gcc/g++/ar/ranlib
    wrapper scripts, USE_SYSCC=1 completed, struct stat/glibc
    header conflict fix.

  - 1384eeb9: clean up static-analysis warnings across 7 files.

  - 249fe497: KERNEL_TC_ISYSTEM_WITH_SYSTEM_CC cmake knob.

  - bbd596d9 / b82f3dc8 / 51ef1f96: boot stage3 linkage: force
    stage3_entry.S first, use .comm rather than .align+.lcomm
    (fix bad alignment), force start.S first for multiboot
    header. Commit bodies document CMake 3.12 GLOB order change
    that exposed the issue.

    --verify-flat-elf and --check-entry-point validators (used
    for the legacy stage3); bootloader now enforces them.

    becomes boot/efi/ and bootloader/ becomes boot/legacy/.
    Pure path moves across 32 files.

## 1814-1827 timer / IRQ / task-wake refactor arc
   (634f90dc..c9f86ff1, Dec 17-18 2018)

Large, focused correctness pass on the scheduler/timer/IRQ code:

  - 634f90dc: tty_setup_for_panic() restarts video only outside
    KD_TEXT.

  - 3fe5f3d5 / c90e1a22: per-task list init helper; debug tasklist
    walker resolves task-wait targets by name.

  - b6993113: off-by-one in set_task_to_wake_after().

  - b732f604: preemption race in IRQ path when an IRQ handler
    re-enabled interrupts *before* EOI.

  - bdea3251: fix stale check in debug_check_tasks_lists() --
    another task could legitimately have moved the sleeping task
    between tick IRQs.

  - 012279e9: set_task_to_wake_after() no longer changes task
    state (caller's responsibility).

  - d362ec66 / 4abf8ba2 / f17b48f3 / 94927643: IRQ cleanups; also
    send EOI to master PIC on spurious slave-PIC IRQ, and always
    EOI before sti.

  - 30412d7e: *reimplement* sleep/wakeup logic with a dedicated
    wake list. -33 net, much simpler.

  - 0f60d7ac: explicit timer_wakeup_list field on task_info.

  - c9f86ff1: extend list unit test (remove coverage +87 lines).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 1828-1841 C11 atomics adoption + sched.h split
   (e22706d8..4be1e8d0, Dec 18-20 2018)

  - e22706d8: **require a C11 compiler** + use <stdatomic.h>.

  - d1f54d10: atomics moved into a dedicated common header.

  - c7c050c5 / bba35661: list helper fix + wake-up logic now
    uses atomic updates.

  - e376f4c9: kernel built with no-FPU-insn codegen (GCC was
    emitting x87 opcodes for atomic_store on long long to avoid
    lock cmpxchg8b; kernel can't afford that).

  - b5439768: stub atomics for GCC <= 4.8 (old Travis box).

  - 2670b45c / abf65a4c / 533d59cd: drop old __sync GCC
    extensions in favour of C11; C++ TUs use std::atomic.

  - f337d75a: fb cursor-blink cosmetic fix.

  - 75a493bc: drop trailing `_enum` typedef style.

  - d02ffd89 / 55d8607e / 4be1e8d0: **split sched.h out of
    process.h** in 3 steps. process.h shrinks by ~130 lines;
    everything scheduler-related is behind sched.h.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 1842-1848 atomic __current / __ticks / __curr_pdir + build
   (6c0aafed..696a84f5, Dec 20-21 2018)

  - b33797f2 / 2c2d8407 / 936cefd6 / 3348d51b: globals renamed
    with `__` prefix and made C11-atomic (__current, __ticks,
    __curr_pdir).

  - 696a84f5: ticks_before_wake_up compresses to 32 bits without
    breaking kernel_sleep (wraparound math in timer handler).

## 1852-1896 kgcov (in-kernel gcov coverage) arc
   (c2cc4de0..5644ad38, Dec 23-27 2018)

New feature: collect gcov coverage data from the *running kernel*
and export it through userspace. Large body of work:

  - c2cc4de0: build the kernel with -fprofile-arcs
    -ftest-coverage when KERNEL_GCOV is on; new kernel/gcov.c
    stub.

  - 9843fe57: call_kernel_global_ctors() -- walk kernel's .ctors
    section (elf.c +41, main.c +3). Needed because gcov emits a
    global ctor per-TU that registers __gcov_info.

  - c2b4d8a0: gcov.c gains GCC's private structs (gcov_fn_info /
    gcov_info / gcov_unit) + __gcov_init() records every TU's
    info so the kernel can later iterate them.

  - 6f46107f / 1df5a97a / 87a0ceac: kernel cmdline parser
    rewrite + factored out; new cmdline.h.

  - e96816a6: unified regular_self_test_end() hook so selftests
    call into se_common on completion.

  - 18bd270b / 043af821: dump-coverage plumbing in single_test_run
    + fix.

  - 3d3cce72 / f7f68e15: scripts/ reorganised into bash_includes/,
    dev/, templates/; generate_coverage_report becomes a template.

  - c12a1777 / 6805fc93 / 66be6440 / 18e012d6 / 3c8b0b87 /
    338539f8 / 1e121b89 / d2c9b9cd / 90b97b06: cmake polish for
    coverage: rename USE_GCOV -> TEST_GCOV, add TOOL_OBJCOPY /
    TOOL_STRIP, add define_env_cache_{bool,str}_var helpers,
    link-script tweaks.

  - b2725516: *Serious* fix: __ticks was a 64-bit atomic that
    happened to work on GCC 6 via cmpxchg8 loop; GCC 7+ moved
    this to libatomic (see Torvald Riegel's "Fix __atomic to
    not implement atomic loads with CAS" patch). __ticks reverts
    to plain u64 + cli/sti around the read.

  - a429186e / 80c686cf / 11e37315 / 4c5be336: CircleCI gets
    debug_syscc_build + debug_clang_build jobs; drops clang
    from Travis; ARCH/CC/CXX become sticky CMake cache vars.

  - 45c04c90 / 39e6d9a2: *complete* the coverage pipeline -- it
    actually works end-to-end from kernel build -> qemu run ->
    devshell dump -> codecov upload on CircleCI.

  - e6cd69af / 9a5a37ce / 9bce2b78 / 8df32705: usermode_apps/
    reorg -- devshell lives under devshellsrc/; `-dcov` flag.

  - 0ea1ff75 / 158dbe50 / 4c8d3e90 / cef582ef: coverage also
    runs for shellcmds; script polish; sys_tilck_run_selftest
    prints function name.

  - 6c3b5cb8: new top-level kernel/syscalls.c (arch-independent);
    renames kernel/arch/i386/syscalls.c -> arch_syscalls.c.

  - 95d1ceeb / 75b39337 / 52f43797 / b432f8f1 / ef328a18 /
    3b7b9550 / 5aca1163 / 13c98cd0 / 5644ad38: gcov transport
    syscalls: TILCK_TESTCMD_GET_NUM_GCOV_FILES,
    sys_gcov_get_file_info, sys_gcov_get_file. Devshell reads
    .gcda sizes + contents from kernel and writes them to disk
    at QEMU-exit time so the outer script can stitch a
    coverage.info.

Net: an ~660-line Tilck-specific mechanism for exporting
libgcov data from ring-0 to userspace, integrated into CI.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 9e9fdafc -- [kgcov][selftests] Fixing synchronization of the
   self tests

Commit: 9e9fdafc24...
Author: Vladislav Valtchev <vladislav.valtchev@gmail.com>
Date:   Thu Dec 27 20:47:06 2018 +0200

Body (excerpted):

  Self-tests must no longer poweroff the VM directly. They must
  return control to the caller when all async tasks they spawned
  are complete, so the devshell can fetch coverage data.

AI notes:

What changed:

  se_common.c, se_kcond.c, se_tasklet.c all gain barrier/
  synchronisation at the end; sys_tilck_run_selftest only
  returns once the test actually wound down (+16 net).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    devshell_coverage.c.

    -- kernel-space was emitting the binary on stdout previously;
    compressed base64 makes it tractable.

    indexing bug (counter->val[j] should have been [k]) that
    mis-reported coverage.
