## 1919-1944 sync/tasklet "special tasks" rework + list typing
   (93ab63da..19492fc6, Jan 4-7 2019)

Major correctness rework of synchronization primitives:

  - 93ab63da / 70ef483e / 70dba6c8: cosmetic renames.

  - **da66d356** (tasklets without kcond): tasklet runners stop
    using kcond. Root cause in the commit body: threaded IRQ
    handlers could change task state while main-code loops were
    iterating state lists, with no safe locking. New axiom:
    "state lists shall never change in interrupt handlers."
    Tasklet runners are declared "special tasks" that do not
    participate in any state list; the runner is woken by an
    atomic state change from enqueue_tasklet_int.

  - **ae8b299c**: after the special-task rule, all the
    cli/sti-while-iterating hacks in sched/kmutex/kcond/process
    can be deleted (-28 net across 8 files).

  - ebafdae3 / c39cecd4 / 77f671a4: small follow-ups
    (is_tasklet_runner helper); remove debug_check_tasks_lists
    (no longer needed).

  - 62854423 / 5ef093b7: new wait_obj.wait_list_node -- every
    wait_obj is now linked into its target's waiter list, so
    wakeups are O(waiters) instead of O(all-sleeping-tasks).

  - c048afb8 / f6848c47: kmutex and kcond rewritten on top of
    wait_list_node.

  - 06f6e59c / b7bb575a / e82a650b / 6f9d3a1e: wait_obj_set/reset
    outlined; preemption disabled inside them; new atomic
    task_set_wait_obj / task_reset_wait_obj combinators.

  - 4a99dc34 / bdc8567b: comment marker + WOBJ_TASK_PTR_ANY_CHILD
    constant.

  - 5a9d528d / 5d2cef47 / 3dce467b / a709ab88: VS Code tasks.json;
    **strong-typed list head**: struct list now a distinct type
    from struct list_node, disambiguating code where one struct
    is both a head and a node. list's members renamed first/last.

  - 0b25e842: build the whole project with
    -fno-strict-aliasing (caught a miscompile in the new list.h
    aliasing). Commit body explains.

  - 24bc4cbb: list-test polish.

  - 87b71380: new task_info->tasks_waiting_list used by kthread
    join path.

  - 19492fc6: system_tests fix (tests weren't detecting non-zero
    exit code under the new 'always-through-devshell' flow).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 83477977 -- [x86_strings] Fix a bug in strlen()

Commit: 45bc497c2e... (actually 83477977) -- small diff but with
a useful cautionary body: plain asm() was unsafe for strlen even
though, in theory, the compiler had all the info to generate
correct code. Switched to asmVolatile() (alt fix with a 2nd
unused variable was worse perf-wise).

## 1957-1973 Debug-all-tasks, GDT refcount, init wait arc
   (c46413bd..3e923ec5, Jan 8-10 2019)

  - c46413bd / c44b2e1c / e457e1ae / 36b63b39 / 96dc47fb:
    Ctrl+F6 dumps all tasks with their state (+names),
    idle_task_kthread renamed to idle.

  - 10f98ff4 / 2121c7a4: reparent children of exiting process to
    init (pid 1), skipping pid==1 itself.

  - c108f7b4: /dev/tty bypasses per-file locking (held across a
    read would prevent signals).

  - d52c91db / a86e54b0: init waits for all tasks now.
    Temporary hack removed once the real fix (next block) lands.

  - 15a3ac84 / c4d3a0f7 / 9f24316d / 264353c0: **GDT entry
    refcount**. Bug: when a parent exited its GDT TLS entries
    were released even though forked children still used them.
    Fix: ref-count per GDT entry; children inc on fork, dec on
    exit. Commit 1971 (a86e54b0) then removes the init hack.

  - e42b94b1: bound the QEMU-poweroff outb loop.

  - a30e9ceb / 3e923ec5: init gets -q and -h; coverage flow no
    longer interfered by orphan-exit messages.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    framebuffer type previously duplicated in efi/legacy.

    adds CONCAT() macro.

    unique varnames.

    cmds_table entries (4 files touched).

## 2019-2025 Serial console on COM1 + TTY alternate charsets + ESC FSM
   (45754c96..c4b68649, Jan 18-20 2019)

  - **45754c96 [serial] Support KB input through COM1 instead
    of PS/2** (+143). New kernel/serial.c adds a COM1 receiver
    path so kernel input can come from serial. kb8042 teaches
    /dev/ttyN about the alternate path.

  - **0010e125 [tty][serial] Actual support for serial console
    on COM1**. serial_mode=2 kernel cmdline makes the tty filter
    pass through raw chars (no cooking), which is what a serial
    terminal wants.

  - 25e6f73b: TERM_SERIAL_ECHO mode dropped (wasn't useful).

  - **a9a2e470 [tty] Support alternate graphic charset
    controlled by shift-out / shift-in** -- SO (0x0E) / SI (0x0F)
    switch in/out of the DEC line-drawing set; ncapp gains a
    demo page.

  - 5a006f3f: ESC ( 0 and ESC ( B sequences (select G0 charset)
    implemented alongside SO/SI.

  - **42ef67a9 [tty] Refactor the ESC seq processing state
    machine** (+130/-108). The ad-hoc switch-case is
    replaced by a proper state enum with per-state handler
    functions.

  - c4b68649: switch-case in tty_term_write_filter() replaced
    by function-pointer dispatch (follow-up to 42ef67a9).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 2026-2041 more tty/term + process/panic fixes before multi-TTY merge
   (2e9bfe03..963d8239, Jan 20-23 2019)

  - 2e9bfe03 / e33fc4ea / e3f282c5: alt_charset table
    readability; CHAR_* renames; additional special chars.

  - **b357f924 [process] Fix an issue with clang release
    builds** -- switch_stack_free_mem_and_schedule() loses the
    stack mid-function; fix frame-pointer handling. Also
    adjusts switch_to_initial_kernel_stack().

  - 60b93ce9 / 9f84d9f0: KERNEL_INITIAL_STACK_SIZE renamed to
    KERNEL_STACK_SIZE; KTHREAD_STACK_SIZE collapsed into same.

  - **cee1e4a5 [selftests] Fix selftest_fault_res_short() for
    clang** -- clang UB-folds `*(int*)0 = x` at compile time, so
    the fault never happens. Switched to bzero() (asm) to defeat
    the optimizer.

  - 050e8615: kthread_exit() was returning through a local
    stack var (freed before use). Fix stores it in a reg.

  - 011ad18e: printk() mishandled `%%` (double-advanced the
    format ptr).

  - f8b5941f / 573cb440: banner regression; debug_show_task_list()
    uses full screen width (+66 lines).

  - bea9ec4b: drop RELEASE row from Travis matrix.

  - e7d33144: Symtab/Strtab bumped to 128K when KERNEL_GCOV=1
    (coverage inflates stab size).

  - 3bdf9c80 / 2274b19b / 963d8239: termtest getopt refactor
    plus a '\n' term bug.

## 2042 MERGE PR #57 - Multi-TTY support (ALT+F1/F2/...)
   (8db8108a, Jan 26 2019, +2586/-1891, 46 files)

Large refactor + feature merge. Body: "long series of patches
implement support in Tilck for multiple TTYs, exactly like on
Linux systems." Key items from the 59-commit branch:

  - **Multi-instance term + tty**: `[term] Support multiple
    instances [part 1..13]` (00cd2753..030a9d9d) and
    `[tty] Support multiple instances [part 1..12]` -- term
    and tty both go from one global struct to arrays indexed by
    tty number.

  - **File layout reorg**: kernel/char/term.c + tty*.c moved
    into kernel/char/tty/ subdirectory (755f0132). New files:
    term.c (763 lines new), term_action_wrappers.c.h,
    tty_ctrl_handlers.c.h, tty_output_default_state.c.h,
    tty_int.h, ttyaux.c. Old kernel/char/term.c (852 lines)
    removed.

  - **`/dev/tty` and `/dev/console`** added (c9985d75);
    `/dev/tty0` special (46742d16); cmdline `-tty_count N` +
    config TTY_COUNT (baedd8ff).

  - **ALT+F1..F12 switch TTY** (aa3a52d5). init.c updated to
    fork a devshell per tty (ec0e4c1e).

  - **VWERASE / delete-word** (ad3784f0). Filter func can
    alter the current char (f275cb77). handle_esc_seq() +
    read_command() in devshell refactored (14cd08d7 / 0a030bab).

  - Devfs can now use arbitrary major numbers (53921589).
    tty + term creation handles OOM without panic (cb4352ee).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 2045 MERGE branch 'warn' - -Wconversion clean across the kernel + CC=clang
   (0684f1e0, Feb 1 2019, +1114/-990, 108 files)

50-commit branch. Subject: "build the kernel with -Wconversion
and CC=clang". Highlights:

  - **[kernel] Build everything with -Wconversion [part 1..36]**:
    a long mechanical series inserting explicit casts and type
    widenings throughout the kernel; most parts touch 3-10 files
    each.

  - **01fe03b1 + 8f2118ea**: new CMake option `WCONV_CLANG` ->
    renamed to `WCONV`, warns if WCONV && compiler != clang. The
    conversion-warning build is effectively a clang-only stress
    test.

  - **703cf2de**: `KERNEL_USE_SYSTEM_CC` renamed to `KERNEL_SYSCC`
    (shorter; this option was introduced in the earlier musl-gcc /
    USE_SYSCC arc).

  - **-Wshadow flip** (15b4fdcd): kernel now built with -Wshadow.

  - **80424d23 MIN3/MAX3 + f3591b38 BOUND(val,min,max)**: new
    macros to replace nested MIN/MAX chains found during the
    -Wconversion sweep.

  - **e0aceaca**: `kernel/pq.c` + pq.h removed (dead code found
    during the audit). -114 lines of priority queue never used.

  - 143cf37f: new `other/errors.cmake` centralizing CMake
    diagnostic messages.

  - 058fbe9d: WCONV scope narrowed to the kernel build only
    (don't impose it on bootloader or userspace).

  - Various small retyping commits: move_cursor / clear_row /
    set_char_at / set_row take u16 args instead of int.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 2046-2050 signals: SIGSEGV / SIGILL / SIGFPE delivery + sys_*kill() stubs
   (a4384e14..f13522ad, Feb 3-4 2019)

  - a4384e14: devshell.h pulls in common/basic_defs.h so tests
    can use the shared types.

  - **23dca1d4 [signals] Introduce signal.c and handle non-cow
    user page fault**. New kernel/signal.c (initial stub) and
    signal.h. User page fault on a non-CoW address now goes
    through signal path instead of panic.

  - **5feb7878 [process] Handle non-CoW user {page, illegal
    opcode} faults properly**. GPF and bad opcode faults now
    terminate the offending user process cleanly. New
    test_signals.c in devshell (+93).

  - **03705995 [process] Send SIGFPE in case of div-by-zero and
    coproc fault**. Divide-by-zero (int 0) and coproc error
    (int 16) deliver SIGFPE; devshell test_signals.c exercises
    both.

  - **f13522ad [syscalls][process] Implement partial support
    for sys_*kill()** (+62 in kernel/syscalls.c). First stubs
    for kill()/tkill()/tgkill() that deliver synchronous
    process-termination on SIGKILL.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 2058-2075 basic signal delivery: SIGINT via Ctrl+C, SIGQUIT via Ctrl+\
   (07f07a95..edb7ec08, Feb 5-7 2019)

Building on 2047-2050, this batch grows signal.c from a stub
into something usable.

  - **07f07a95**: zero-map user-stack pages with CoW when
    MMAP_NO_COW=0 (prerequisite for test programs that actually
    touch their stack after a signal).

  - **33d25dad / 2adb509c / ebe7621d** `[signal]
    Working on basic signal support [parts 1-3]`. Signal
    delivery plumbing: per-task pending mask, `struct sigaction`,
    sigprocmask(), sigaction(), sigreturn() skeleton. Grows
    kernel/signal.c from 11 to ~240 lines across the three
    parts.

  - 2a0696b9: tty echoes special control chars (`^C`, `^\\`)
    when ISIG is on.

  - **f2fc5e4d / 7f4b896b**: Ctrl+C now sends SIGINT.
    7f4b896b moves the kb/tty -> foreground-group dispatch
    into tty_ctrl_handlers.c.h and records "Ctrl+C works for
    the first time".

  - **0fa2aadc**: expose `kernel_process_pi` so init_tty() can
    assign ttys before init is created (previously circular).

  - **d2f9140f**: minimal setsid() + TIOCSCTTY ioctl so init
    can allocate a controlling tty per instance. init.c's
    per-tty fork loop uses it.

  - e3172945 / 04f23829 / a5cc1fd1: duplicate_user_argv() bug
    -- copy_str_array_from_user() doesn't track `*written`
    offsets the way callers expected. Fixed in user.c instead
    of the call sites.

  - 185bf88f / 4b53c206 / 5cd37007 / edb7ec08: devshell
    shell-ignores SIGINT/SIGQUIT (423b69ba init too); ringbuf
    gets a reset helper; tty resets kb input on Ctrl+C;
    Ctrl+\ delivers SIGQUIT.

  - bb1a90b1: cosmetic tweaks to count_lines_of_code.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 2091-2100 sys_times() + syscall reorg; select() starts
   (c99635dc..87f52085, Feb 8-13 2019)

  - c99635dc: sys_*kill() implementations moved together.

  - **d1d72d46 [syscalls] Introduce a minimal implementation
    of sys_times()**: returns per-process user/sys tick
    counters via struct tms.

  - 8c540133: syscalls.md refreshed with signals + times.

  - 80c22c4a / ac1dd9b4: tty micro-opts; banner moved to use
    ANSI sequences so serial-console system tests see it as
    plain text.

  - **58ded4f3 [select] Start working on select(): debug print
    its arguments**. New kernel/select.c (+68) that only
    parses/prints fd_set, then early-returns. First step of
    the select() implementation arc.

  - **4a4b1681 / 40bc0e60 / f4b2f060 [kcond][select][wobj]
    Working on the support for a multi_wait_obj**. New wobj.c
    (+62 then +50 then +23), kmutex.c shrinks by 70. The
    multi-object waiter is the primitive select() will stand
    on; kmutex loses the multi-wait glue because wobj.c now
    owns it. se_mobj_waiter.c self-test (+75) proves it.

  - **87f52085 [vfs] Introduce vfs_read_ready() and
    vfs_write_ready()**: the vfs-level poll primitive select()
    will call for each monitored fd.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 2101-2115 select() implementation + TTY read-ready + temp-kernel-alloc
   (df07d86e..ebf4d476, Feb 13-14 2019)

  - df07d86e / 20181fb7 / e1efd1ca `[select] Working on a
    select() implementation [part 1-2/2]`: full select() path
    wired up. fd_set parsing, per-fd waiters registered via
    multi_obj_waiter, sleep with optional timeout via the
    timer-handler's expiration queue. +142 then +134 on
    kernel/select.c.

  - **d6d96bae [tty] Support read_ready()**: tty gets a
    byte-available predicate so select() on /dev/tty can
    wait for a complete input line without spinning.

  - 0b9d8874 / 49b8fe95: termtest test probes
    (sleep_then_read, sym_read) to exercise the select path.

  - 1fa5a9a4: comment out the debug-print in select(); it was
    only useful during bring-up.

  - e1f030c4: TCSAFLUSH now actually drops pending input
    (previously only toggled modes).

  - 4fab6489: devshell -Wformat fixes.

  - c6ac643e / 6f470ce1: select refactor; new select2 devshell
    test; test_misc.c split out (+181).

  - 7721acac: move a few process-related constants from
    process.h into common/config.h (userapps can see them).

  - edf5e037: generic bintree compare returns signed ptr
    (sptr) instead of int, for future-proofing.

  - **78d89d94 [process] Introduce task_temp_kernel_{alloc,
    free}()**: a per-task arena tied to the task's lifetime.
    When a task dies, everything it allocated here gets
    released automatically. +79 kernel/process.c.

  - ebf4d476: tty_kb_buf_reset() missed resetting
    end_line_delim_count.

## 2116-2125 mobj_waiter uses temp-alloc; sys_select() 6-part refactor
   (d6edf0af..ae3f590c, Feb 14-15 2019)

  - d6edf0af: allocate/free mobj_waiter() via
    task_temp_kernel_alloc() instead of kmalloc -- it's
    naturally per-task and per-call.

  - b5a2f297: init the bintree node when the alloc comes from
    the temp arena (zero-init isn't enough).

  - **8d07db4a..93836123 `[select] Refactoring sys_select()
    [parts 1-6/6]`**: structural rewrite of sys_select() into
    helper functions (build waiter list, walk fds, poll each
    fd_set, compute timeout, sleep, rebuild returned fd_set).
    Net file size stays roughly the same (+79/-65, etc.) but
    the function becomes composable.

  - **7d6c5db3 [process] Free all temp kernel allocs in
    terminate_process()**: ensures task_temp_kernel_* arena is
    drained on exit.

  - **ae3f590c [process] Fix a bug in terminate_process()
    when ti != curr**: we were calling
    set_page_directory(get_kernel_pdir()) unconditionally, so
    a task calling sys_kill(other_pid) would swap its own
    pdir to the kernel pdir and fault on return.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 2126-2135 count_lines_of_code rework + signal/SIG_IGN hack refinement
   (b044d0df..81e0d4a7, Feb 15-18 2019)

  - b044d0df..2a251644: seven-part rewrite of
    scripts/dev/count_lines_of_code. The script ends up much
    more structured -- per-language buckets, extensible table,
    JSON-style output for README.

  - **f752a8d2 [signal] Hack: silently replace custom sig.
    handlers with SIG_IGN**: user-installed handlers not yet
    supported, so accept them but install SIG_IGN under the
    hood, so programs using signal() at least don't die.

  - **81e0d4a7 [signal] Hack: treat custom handlers as
    SIG_IGN but keep their value**. Body explains why: the
    previous hack swapped the slot to SIG_IGN, but fork copies
    SIG_IGN (stays ignored) while custom handlers should reset
    to SIG_DFL in the child. Keeping the stored value preserves
    that semantic.

  - 75025704: syscalls.md minor update.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 2136-2145 TTY CSI rework: ESC ( / ESC ), per-CSI fn table, BOLD/reverse
   (721a0c93..a7d3f64e, Feb 19-25 2019)

  - 721a0c93: ESC [ n d (VPA -- cursor to row) plus misc
    fixes discovered while working on ncapp demo.

  - **c5dab400 [tty] Implement properly support for ESC(?,
    ESC)? controlling transl tables** (+318 lines in
    tty_output.c). G0/G1 selection, SI/SO toggling which
    translation table is active, and per-tty translation state
    stored in struct tty.

  - f0d7fb56: ESC c (RIS) now actually resets the tty (was
    a no-op).

  - **5d0b8015 / cf2b312d**: BOLD/BRIGHT attribute actually
    distinguishes intensity; reverse + bright composites
    correctly (was colliding with the same bit).

  - 31bc0c9a: chdir() returns -ENOTDIR when the target isn't
    a directory (was silently succeeding).

  - **4ed4169f..0a6f0e0d `[tty] Move CSI handlers from
    switch-case to function pointer table [1-3]`**: the big
    CSI switch in tty_output.c is replaced by a dispatch
    table keyed by the final byte. Matches the pattern used
    earlier for the ESC FSM.

  - a7d3f64e: translation tables moved into
    tty_output_default_state.c.h and compressed (-133).

## 2146-2158 more TTY polish: CSI HPA, reset helper, \b / \t / \v / \f
   (e8b0c6a3..78717ded, Feb 25-26 2019)

  - e8b0c6a3: tty_default_trans_table made ~4x more compact
    (-95).

  - 05e094f3: Ctrl+[ now emits ^[ (ESC) and behaves as ESC.

  - 12fb2dfe: Ctrl+O no longer intercepted -- lets user
    programs receive raw shift-in.

  - 5876c671 / 517d3b1c: `attrs` field moves from
    twfilter_ctx to struct tty (tty-scoped, not
    filter-scoped); KB_INPUT_BS relocated to config.h.

  - **a63cc8b2 [tty] Allow ESC to restart seq and support
    0x9B as CSI: ESC [**: receiving ESC mid-sequence restarts,
    and 0x9B (8-bit CSI) is accepted as an alias for `ESC [`.

  - 8f33f5c6: HPA (ESC [ n `) -- move to column, same row.

  - a162b244 / c0d9653d: tty_reset_filter_ctx() helper;
    `term_write_filter_ctx_t` -> `twfilter_ctx_t` (shorter).

  - dc3fefc6: \b (backspace) was broken in the default-state
    handler.

  - f8bf898a: CSI ABCD (cursor move) `d[4]` array retyped
    signed (int parameters can be negative).

  - e3082b3b: \t was being handled in tty_input (wrong
    layer); moved to default_state so output sees it.

  - 78717ded: \v and \f now behave like raw linefeeds
    (previously dropped).

    devices** (+79). Four ttyS nodes in devfs, init spawns a
    shell on ttyS0 when serial console is active.

  - 34a8227e: term no longer allocates the scroll buffer when
    `serial_port_fwd != 0` (output goes straight to the serial
    port).

  - **6e4f9a2c [tty] Making backspace to work in the serial

    (three-char erase sequence xterm uses).

  - 009e4352: poll/select path for /dev/tty{1..N} (via ttyaux)
    was broken by the earlier ttyaux split.

    TTYS0_MINOR named constant.

    preemption disabled**: drivers are not reentrant; move to
    preempt-disabled init path.

  - 8a039fe2: serial RX loop moved into the bottom-half
    handler (was running in interrupt ctx).

    se_mobj_waiter more robust; wobj bzero() length bug.

  - **2f8d54bd [tty][serial] Fix support for ttyS{0,1,2,3}

    `-initrd` glue; busybox launch.json; termtest reproduces a
    bug triggered by ASH; poll() gets a pre-sleep ready scan.

  - **07fe6e48 [select] Two fixes: as poll: first check ready,
    and no wait when {0,0} timeout**: aligns select() with
    poll() + implements the specified POSIX behavior when
    tv=={0,0} (non-blocking poll).

  - **a928f69e [process32] Call arch_specific_free_task() in
    create_usermode_task()** -- body is informative:
    execve() must clear all GDT/LDT entries, otherwise ASH's
    set_thread_area() returns -ESRCH because a stale entry is
    still bound to an old process. Unblocks busybox ash.

## 2204-2217 ringbuf split: safe_ringbuf (reentrant) + simpler ringbuf
   (94bea58a..642e754c, Mar 18 2019)

This arc splits the old `ringbuf` into two variants: the
reentrant one (kept for tasklet + term cross-ctx use) becomes
`safe_ringbuf`, and a new simpler non-reentrant ringbuf is
introduced for the single-owner cases.

  - 94bea58a..ff387665 `[ringbuf] Rename ringbuf* ->
    safe_ringbuf*` (header, .c, callers).

  - **f42c181c [ringbuf] Introduce a simpler non-reentrant
    ringbuf implementation** (+109 ringbuf.c, +36 header).

  - c09ef9b0: elem-count replaces full-flag.

  - **3e293c62 [ringbuf] Intr. a unit tests for ringbuf**
    (+194 unittests/ringbuf_test.cpp) and fix a subtle
    ringbuf_unwrite bug.

  - 71c8b8d7: tty input switches from safe_ringbuf to plain
    ringbuf (only the tty's own tasklet writes it).

  - f7497c01: drop unwrite/reset from safe_ringbuf variant
    (unused).

  - 413aeab7 / 6996b1d8 / 1d3fd2f2 / 912dfa04 / 177ca2b8:
    rename cascade -- kb_input_* -> input_*; KB_INPUT_BS ->
    TTY_INPUT_BS.

  - 642e754c: write_ex removed; read1/write1/reset inlined
    in header.

## 2218-2225 IRQ sharing + PIC split; init_drivers() back to preempt-enabled
   (32b79e15..0667d8f7, Mar 19-20 2019)

  - **32b79e15 [irq] Support IRQ sharing** (+120/-47). IRQs
    get a handler list rather than a single function pointer;
    serial.c + 8042 register onto it so multiple devices on
    the same IRQ work. interrupts.h grows with the shared
    return codes (`IRQ_HANDLED`, `IRQ_NOT_HANDLED`).

  - 6c6595ce / 82f08acd: irq_handlers_list rename; new
    `list_for_each_ro` macro for read-only list walks, used
    in 6 places.

  - **32d9a6cd [irq] Move PIC-specific stuff in a separate
    .c.h file**: kernel/arch/i386/irq.c shrinks by 130 lines;
    new `pic.c.h` holds 8259-specific code. Prep for APIC
    support later.

  - 8f2da85c: tweak RGB of the 16 fb-console colors
    (readability on black background).

  - **3e281217 [main] Create the first process (pid 1) in
    SLEEPING state**: body explains: this lets init_drivers()
    run preempt-enabled in a kernel thread without risking a
    user process preempting driver init. Replaces the
    c7f3e797 workaround with a cleaner solution.

  - 986f5545 / 0667d8f7: kmain init path renames for
    consistency (`init_X_drivers`, `init_X_subsys` pattern).

## 2276-2296 process_info split; panic hardening; fault_resumable asm bug
   (2a25126f..c0f9d713, Mar 25-26 2019)

A large rework of the task/process data model and panic path
in the run-up to POSIX process-groups and sessions.

  - 2a25126f / 36d45e9f: PS/2 kb driver bug; task_set_wait_obj
    runs with preemption disabled.

  - 8f13b422 / 44c5a4c9 / e496fa2b: devshell polish +
    gen_other_builds tweaks.

  - **c6e6517a [tty][syscalls] Preparations for process groups
    and sessions support** (+64 tty_ioctl.c): stubs for
    setpgid/getpgrp/setsid plumbing.

  - 4b3147c7 / 2497725e / 7f0853a5 / 54e92ab6 / b3740692 /
    725a7769 / 7d016128: **task_info / process_info struct
    split work**. siblings_node, pid, and process-level fields
    move from task_info to process_info. New helper
    `is_main_thread(ti)`; `did_call_execve` flag for later use.
    Reorders both structs to pack hot fields together.

  - ddd9f99a: kthread_exit() sets `curr = kernel_process`
    (safer for the next schedule).

  - **476140bf / 638c9a7b**: ASSERT(curr != NULL) in both
    switch_to_task_pop_nested_interrupts() and switch_to_task();
    removes dead "curr might be null" branches.

  - **56140e4f [process] Refactoring sys_fork()** (+59/-51):
    splits into helpers so the happy-path is readable.

  - **193c491a / 689b9770 [panic] Better handling of page
    fault in panic state**: panic no longer crashes recursively
    when it hits a bad pointer in an argv; also handles
    curr == NULL and kernel_process == NULL (very early).

  - 39aa3eb7: set_current_task() -> set_curr_task() rename.

  - **c0f9d713 [panic] Further improving panic handling**
    (+93/-73 panic.c, +42/-27 idt.c): reshapes panic() as a
    state machine that can nest one level without looping.

## 2304-2313 sys_mmap pg-count fix; partial munmap; restore KD_TEXT on die
   (3dd53a7f..f2495b37, Mar 27 2019)

  - **3dd53a7f [mm] Fix in sys_mmap_pgoff()**: was passing
    byte count as `page_count` to process_add_user_mapping().
    Could allow much more memory to be mapped than requested.

  - 422b0cb7 / 206a7b8e: minor refactoring.

  - **d52d3955 [signal][process] Make send_signal() to run
    with preemption disabled**: body explains: to safely deref
    a target pid (the process could exit between pid lookup
    and access), send_signal() takes TID and acquires the
    task_info* under preempt-disable.

  - 370b0997: setsid()/getsid() move out of tty_ioctl into
    process.c (not tty-specific).

  - **89c118bf [process] Panic on init's termination**: init
    dying used to silently loop; now BUG().

  - aba3b349: terminate_process() refactored (+41/-20).

  - **57adfbb5 [process_mm] Handle partial unmap in
    sys_munmap()**: unmap within a larger mapping now splits
    rather than failing.

  - **787b5ca8 [process][vfs] Make vfs_close() to remove all
    mappings to that handle**: close() of an mmap()ed fd now
    tears the mapping down automatically.

  - **f2495b37 [fbdev][tty] Restore TTY in KD_TEXT mode if
    the last prog mapping fb dies**: if a fb client crashes
    while tty is KD_GRAPHICS, the kernel flips back to KD_TEXT
    so future output is visible.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 2314-2325 paging vocabulary rework; __current NON-atomic
   (0968ead1..0d9f3ad4, Mar 29-31 2019)

Follow-ups to the long "atomics" arc from 2018: reversing the
over-cautious `atomic_*` use where Tilck's single-CPU model
guarantees non-tearing.

  - **0968ead1 [kmutex][kcond] Make global id counters
    NON atomic**. Body: Tilck is UP, init_k{mutex,cond} never
    runs in interrupt context, so relaxed atomic was overkill.

  - **9b1fde7f [paging][x86_utils] Remove __curr_pdir by
    reading directly CR3 instead**: `cpu_read_cr3()` helper is
    cheaper than an atomic load of a mirror variable.

  - 51774cd4: set_page_directory() -> set_curr_pdir() rename.

  - efea7874: new still_using_orig_pdir() helper for
    system_mmap.c.

  - 4d804f33: unmmap_pages() moved into paging.c (belongs
    there).

  - **84c365f5 [paging] Rename page_directory_t to pdir_t**
    (13 files). Shorter name, matches the `set_curr_pdir`
    naming.

  - **3ecd8854 [process][sched] Make `__current` NON-atomic**
    with an explanation comment in get_curr_task(): single-CPU
    invariant + a word-sized write is naturally atomic on
    every arch Tilck targets.

  - ac3b0cdc: comments in process32.c + timer_handler.

  - ec1dd9dd: new `gcc_fast_rel` entry in
    adv/gen_other_builds.

  - 92b87242: cmake gets a PERMISSIVE env-var escape hatch
    because CMake mangles CC paths non-deterministically,
    breaking the "don't reconfigure with a different CC" check.

  - 78290c25: `pid_t` replaced with plain `int` throughout
    (avoided typedef-chasing).

  - 0d9f3ad4: README update.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 2342-2350 TID-returning kthread_create; preempt-safe get_task;
              waitpid refactor; fbcon PSF + scroll-cursor fix
   (dec3d891..be63a8d4, Apr 3-4 2019)

  - **dec3d891 [process] Make kthread_create() to return a
    TID, not a task_info* pointer**: returning a raw pointer
    was unsafe once preemption is re-enabled.

  - **78c65c69 [process][sched] Make get_task() callable
    only with preemption disabled**. Body explains the
    rationale: no ref-count, so the task can die while the
    caller holds the pointer. All callers updated.

  - **fad8c3f8 [process] Refactor sys_waitpid in a way to
    avoid unsafe access to tasks** (+68/-50): same issue as
    above -- waitpid now snapshots needed fields under
    preempt-disable.

  - bbe95d81: new `struct k_rusage` in sys_types.h.

  - 0aaee5da / 8cc15c74: README + termtest polish.

  - **5f3b44be [fb_console] Don't use struct psf2_header in
    the code** (+93/-108): replace direct PSF2 struct use
    with a font abstraction -> enables PSF1 support next.

  - **36c33867 [handle_fault][panic] Call printk() in a safe
    way in case of fault in panic**: uses the varargs-safe
    wrapper from 27acfac0.

  - **be63a8d4 [fb_console] Fix a long-lasting bug with
    cursor movement**: term hides the cursor by moving it
    off-screen; fb_console lacked OOB checks and crashed with
    `termtest -p` at specific resolutions.

## 2393-2406 ramfs born; FS ref-count; -ENOTDIR semantics
   (ea92f5dc..d2e93934, Apr 7-8 2019)

  - **ea92f5dc [ramfs] Working on filesystem's skeleton**
    (+107 kernel/fs/ramfs.c).

  - **17dfe2a7 [vfs][mp] Introduce a per-filesystem
    ref-count**: fs/mp.c gains vfs_{inc,dec}_ref_count so
    mounted filesystems can't be unmounted while in use.

  - 3b31b3a0: vfs_dup() increments fs ref-count.

  - a34902be: devfs + fat32 save open() flags; write() on
    read-only handle returns -EBADF.

    inodes, multi-component paths like /a/b/c.

  - **ec279da7 [vfs][fat32][devfs][ramfs] Return -ENOTDIR
    when appropriate**: body explains the fix -- `f1/` used
    to be treated as `f1`; now it correctly errors if `f1`
    isn't a directory. Implemented in all four filesystems.

    (-ENOENT for empty path).

## 2407-2420 execve re-architected: load with preemption enabled
   (eaef3379..<later>, Apr 8-9 2019)

  - **eaef3379 [execve] Switch to the new task's image when
    curr != kernel_process** (+36/-13): sets up the new pdir
    before ELF load, not after.

  - 12ac579d / f70694af: execve_prepare_process() split out;
    new first_execve() for init (distinct code path without
    killing a "caller" task).

  - **654ad835 [elf] Refactoring load_elf_program()**
    (+91/-70): reshape into phases.

  - 689e100f: ELF loader stops writing into the new image's
    vaddr space (temp-maps scratch pages instead).

  - **1fca21c9 [execve][elf][process32] Run load_elf_program()
    with preemption enabled**: the culmination of the arc --
    ELF loading no longer blocks other tasks.

  - d681e14d / 61d499b2: cosmetics.

  - ed8341f8 / 4f70410b: scheduler uses `curr_int` (interrupt
    context curr) instead of per-IRQ `curr_irq`;
    switch_to_idle_task_outside_interrupt_context() replaced
    by schedule_outside_interrupt_context().

## 2417-2426 /sbin/init runs from init-drivers kthread; fslock; syscall int
   (dca8b844..172e9231, Apr 9-10 2019)

  - **dca8b844 [main][execve] Run /sbin/init after init
    drivers**. Body is important: the prior approach ran init
    directly in kmain(), which forced ELF-load with
    preemption disabled. Moving init to a kthread after
    driver init lets all VFS calls run with preemption on.
    kmain.c shrinks by 53 lines.

  - 916a1235: build_fatpart avoids duplicating /sbin/init in
    /bin.

  - 8318af0e / 125eafe3: STATIC_KMUTEX_INIT() /
    STATIC_KSEM_INIT() / STATIC_KCOND_INIT() macros.

  - **da4e437a [mp] Use a kmutex instead of disabling
    preemption**: fine-grained mp-table lock replaces the
    global preempt-disable.

  - **1b03fc29 [process] Introduce process_info->fslock**
    (+153/-101 fs_syscalls.c): per-process lock on the fd
    table, replacing ad-hoc preempt-disable around FD ops.

  - **f3064f83 / 30f73fe0 [syscalls] Use `int` as return
    type for almost all the syscalls [part 1-2]**. Body
    explains: Linux syscalls return values fitting in 32-bit
    int even on 64-bit systems (read/write cap at
    0x7ffff000); the exceptions are sys_brk and
    sys_mmap_pgoff.

  - 8fbaeefd: fds typed as int (not u32).

  - **172e9231 [vfs] Make all VFS calls except dup and close
    to require preemption enabled**: new NOT_IN_INT_HANDLER
    assertion that catches regressions.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
